translations.source: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Magungunan Gargajiya da na Ƙari da Haɗaɗɗun Magunguna Bayani: Maganin gargajiya Maganin gargajiya yana da dogon tarihi. Shi ne jimillar ilimi da ƙwarewa da ayyuka bisa ƙa'idoji da imani da gogewa na cikin gida ga mutane masu al’adu daban-daban, wanda za a iya bayaninsa da wanda ba za a iya ba, wanda ake amfani da su wajen kula da lafiya da kuma rigakafi da gano cuta da ingantawa ko magance rashin lafiyar jiki da ta ƙwaƙwalwa. Maganin Ƙari "Maganin ƙari " ko "maganin madadi " na nufin wani jerin tsarin ayyukan kula da lafiya waɗanda ba sa cikin al'adar ƙasar na kanta ko magungunan gargajiya kuma ba sa cikin gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya. Ana amfani da su a madadin magungunan gargajiya a wasu ƙasashe. Magungunan ganye Magungunan ganye sun haɗa da ganyayyaki da kayan ganyayyaki da gyara ganyayyayki da kuma kayan ganye da aka sarrafa, waɗanda ke ƙunshe da sassan tsirrai ko wasu kayan shuka ko duka. Tasiri: An ƙirƙira kuma an ƙaddamar da Dabarun Magungunan Gargajiya na WHO 2014-2023 don mayar da martani ga ƙudurin Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya kan magungunan gargajiya (WHA62.13).

translations.source: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners; To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.reference: Dabarun na da nufin tallafawa ƙasashe mambobin ƙungiyar wuein samar da tsare-tsare na gaske da aiwatar da tsare-tsaren ayyuka waɗanda za su ƙarfafa rawar da magungunan gargajiya ke takawa wajen kiyaye lafiyar al'umma. Magance ƙalubalen, mayar da martani ga buƙatun da ƙasashe mambobi suka gano, da kuma haɓaka aikin da aka yi a ƙarƙashin dabarun maganin gargajiya na WHO: 2002-2005, dabarun da aka sabunta a lokacin 2014-2023 ya ba da ƙaimi fiye da wanda ya gabace shi don ba da fifiko ga ayyuka da tsare-tsaren kiwon lafiya, waɗanda suka haɗa magungunan gargajiya da na ƙari, da ayyuka masu aiki. Manufofin dabarun su ne: Don gina fage na ilimi don gudanar da aikin T&CM ta hanyar tsare-tsare na ƙasa waɗanda suka dace; Don ƙarfafa tabbatar da inganci da aminci da amfani da T & CM yadda ya dace kuma cikin tasiri ta hanyar kula da magunguna da ayyuka da masu aiki; Don haɓaka shiga ko ina a duniya ta hanyar haɗe ayyukan T&CM cikin gudanar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kula da lafiyar kai. Martanin WHO: an samar da rahoton na WHO na duniya game da maganin gargajiya da na ƙari na 2019 don cike giɓin bayanai abin dogaro, sahihi, kuma na hukuma daga Ƙasashe Mambobi na yankin T&CM. Wannan rahoto ya yi duba kan ci gaban duniya a T&CM cikin shekaru ashirin da suka gabata, kuma ya dogara ne kan gudummawar ƙasashe mambobin WHO guda 179. Ya ba da bayanai masu mahimmanci ga masu tsara manufofi, da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya, da jama'a don yin amfani da yiwuwar gudummawar T&CM ga lafiya da walwala.

translations.source: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs. The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.reference: Dashe Bayani: Dasa tantanin halitta da tishu da gaɓoɓin mutane Dashentantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin jikin mutum na ceton rayuka da yawa kuma yana dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci inda babu tasiri madadin su. Cikin shekaru 50, dashe yayi nasara a faɗin duniya. Sai dai akwai babban bambanci tsakanin ƙasashe wajen samun damar dashen da ya dace da kuma matakin aminci da inganci da samun gudummawa da dashen tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu da gaɓoɓi. Abubuwan da suka dace na dashe su ne kan gaba. Musamman, rashin biyan buƙatun marasa lafiya da ƙarancin abin dashe na haifar da yiwuwar fataucin sassan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Dashen mutum Tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu don dasawa na iya ceton rayuka ko dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci. Misali corneal graft zai iya dawo da gani a makantar corneal;

A cikin shekaru 50, kera sababbin sashi ya zama aikin yaduwa a duniya. Duk da haka, akwai bambance-bambancen babban a tsakanin kasashe kan samun abubuwan da za'a kera sababbin sashi da kuma matakin tsaro, inganci, tasiri na bayar da abubuwan jiki da kera sababbin sashi. translations.source: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation. Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.reference: dashen tantanin halitta na hematopoietic na iya warkar da cututtukan da aka haifi mutum da su ko waɗanda suka samu, waɗanda suka haɗa da wasu daga cikin cutar sankarar bargo. Duba ƙarin bayani akan Tantanin Halitta na Haematopoeitic Sau da yawa dashen bawul na zuciyar ɗan adam yakan zama mafi kyawun yanayin maye gurbi kuma marasa lafiya masu karɓa ba sa buƙatar dogon lokaci na maganin cututtukan sarƙewar jini. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, wato daga dabba zuwa mutum, shi ne rayayyen tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin asali na dabba da ruwan jikin mutum da tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin da ke da ex vivo da waɗannan masu ran, da kayan xenogeneic, suna da yiwuwar zama madadin kayan asali na jikin ɗan adam da kuma cike giɓin da ke cikin kayan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Gudunmawa: Ya kamata a haɓaka gudummawa daga matattu zuwa iyakar ƙarfinsu don gujewa haɗarin da ke tattare da masu ba da gudummawa gwargwadon iko, kamar yadda aka bayyana a cikin Ƙa’idodin Jagora na 3. Akwai buƙata ga al'ummomi da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya ta samun ilimi mai zurfi game da gudummawa da dashe, kuma wannan shi ne mabuɗin samun nasarar shirye-shiryen bayar da gudummawa daga waɗanda da suka mutu. Duk da haka, duk da yawan amfani da kayan da aka ba da gudummawa daga matattu, gudummawar da ake bayarwa daga masu ba da gudummawar rayuwa ya zama dole don wasu nau'ikan dashe ko don rama ƙarancin kayan da ake samu daga masu ba da gudummawar da suka mutu don biyan buƙatun majiyyata. Don haka, ana ba da gudummawar rayuwa duk da cewa tana ƙunshe da haɗari ga mai ba da gudummawa wanda ba za a yi sakaci da shi ba. Yayin da sayan kayan ɗan adam na dashe daga masu bayarwa ga matattu ko masu rai, kuma dashen allogeneic na gaba na iya haifar da haɗari na ɗabi'a da aminci ga duka masu karɓa da mai ba da gudummawa da tsauraran kula da ingantaccen sa ido ya kamata hukumomin lafiya su aiwatar da su don kare su.

**Hausa Translation:**

Kwararawa na sel daya na kwayoyin hawan jini (haematopoietic stem cells) na iya magance cutan hawan jini da aka haifa ko kuma suka samu, gami da wasu cututtukan leucaemia.  Kalli karin bayani akan Kwayoyin Hawan Jini.  Kwararawa na zuƙin mutum (human heart valve) sau da yawa ya zama mafi kyawun maye gurbin, kuma marasa lafiya da suka samu wannan kwararawa ba sa buƙatar kula da jinin su na dogon lokaci (anti-coagulation therapy).

Xenotransplantasyon: Xenotransplantasyon, wato kwararawa daga dabbobi zuwa mutane, an bayyana ta a matsayin sel, ginshiƙi, ko organ na asalin dabbobi zuwa jinin mutum, sel, ginshiƙi, ko organ da aka yi aiki a kansu a waje da jiki (ex vivo) tare da waɗannan abubuwan da suka zo daga dabbobi (xenogeneic).  Wannan yana da damar zama a matsayin madadin abubuwan da suka fito daga mutane, kuma ya haɗa ƙarancin abubuwan da ke nan don kwararawa.

Bashi:  Bashi daga mutanen da suka mutu ya kamata a haɓaka zuwa mafi yawan ƙarfin magani, ya rage haɗin gaske ga masu ba da bayin jinin da suka yi rai, kamar yadda aka bayyana a Daruruwan WHO na 3.  An gano buƙatar al'umma da ƙwararrun lafiya su sami ilimi game da bashi da kwararawa, kuma shi ne babban abin da ke kawo nasarar shirye-shiryen bashi daga mutanen da suka mutu.  Koyawa, duk da amsar da ake samu daga abubuwan da aka bashi daga mutanen da suka mutu, bashi daga mutanen da suka yi rai yana da buƙata don wasu nau'o'in kwararawa ko don warware kawar da abubuwan da ke nan daga mutanen da suka mutu don biyan buƙatun marasa lafiya.  Don haka, ana yin bashi daga mutanen da suka yi rai, duk da cewa yana haɗa haɗin gaske ga mai ba da bayin jinin waɗanda ba sa zama ƙaƙƙarawa.

Kamar yadda samun abubuwan jikin mutum don kwararwa daga mutanen da suka mutu ko da suka yi rai da kwararwar allogeneic na iya haɗa haɗin laifi da tsaro ga mai karɓar kwararawa da mai ba da bayin jinin, hukumomin lafiya dole ne su aiwatar da tsarin ƙaƙƙarrawa da kuma kula da aiki yadda ya kamata don kare su.

translations.source: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.reference: Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun ba da umarni mai kyakkyawar kulawa ga masu bayarwa da masu karɓa. Sa ido a fili na hukumomin lafiya kan ayyukan bayar da agaji da ayyukan dashe yana da matuƙar muhimmanci wajen ƙara amincewar jama'a kan tsarin. Bugu da ƙari, sau da dama yanke shawarar zama mai bayarwa ya dogara ne kan fahimtar cewa gudummawar da aka samu don samar da dashen zai iya amfanar da buƙatun lafiyar iyalin mai bayarwa. Idan mun shirya karɓar dasshe idan muna da buƙatar hakan, to ya kamata mu kasance a shirye don bayarwa Ɗa'a: Ƙa'idodin Jagora na WHO kan Dashen Tantanin halitta da Tishu da Gaɓoɓin Jiki A 1987, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta arba'in, ta damu da kasuwanci don riba kan sassan jikin mutum, ta fara shirye-shiryen Ƙa'idodin Jagora na farko na WHO game da dashe, wanda Majalisar ta amince da shi a 1991 a ƙuduri na WHA44.25. Waɗannan Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun yi tasiri sosai ga ƙa'idodin ƙwararru da ayyuka da kuma dokoki a duk duniya cikin kusan shekaru ashirin. Bayan tuntuɓa wadda ta ɗauki shekaru da yawa, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta Sittin da Uku ta amince da ƙuduri na WHA63.22 a ranar 21 ga Mayu 2010, tare da amincewa da sabunta Ƙa'idodin Jagororanci na WHO da gano wuraren ci gaba don inganta ayyukan ba da gudummawa da dasawa. Sanarwar Istanbul Ƙwararru na ba da gudummawa da dasawa daga dukan yankuna, ta hanyar ƙungiyoyi da cibiyoyi da yawa, suna goyon bayan Sanarwar Istanbul game da fataucin sassan jiki da yawon shaƙatawa da aka bunƙasa a ƙarƙashin jagorancin ƙungiyar dashe da kuma Ƙungiya Nephrology ta Ƙasa-da-ƙasa a watan Mayun 2008.

Idan mun shirya karɓar janyewa (transplant) idan muna bukata, to ya kamata mu shirya ba da abubuwan ciki.

*Dabarun Da Ke Da Daraja na WHO akan Janyewa (Transplant) na Kwayoyin Halitta, Kwantittukaka da Organai*

A shekarar 1987, Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta duniya ta 40, ta damu da cinikin organai na mutane don samun kudi, ta fara shirye-shiryen *Misakainiyoyin Da Ke Da Daraja na WHO* akan janyewa (transplant), wanda Majalisar ta amince da shi a shekarar 1991 a bisa ga ƙudirin WHA44.25. Waɗannan *Misakainiyoyin Da Ke Da Daraja* sun ƙara ƙarfafawa ƙudade da ayyukan ƙwararru da kuma dokokin a duniya a cikin kusan shekaranni biyu. Bayan tsarin tattaunawa da ya ɗauki shekaru da yawa, Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta duniya ta 63 ta amince da ƙudirin WHA63.22 a ranar 21 ga Mayu, shekarar 2010, wanda ya amince da *Misakainiyoyin Da Ke Da Daraja na WHO* da sabuntawa da kuma gano fannoni na ci gaba don ƙara ƙaƙauyar bayar da abubuwan ciki da janyewa (transplant).

*Sanarwar Istanbul*

Kwararru akan bayar da abubuwan ciki da janyewa (transplant) daga dukkan yankuna, ta hanyar kungiyoyi da jami'ai da yawa, suna amincewa da *Sanarwar Istanbul* akan kasuwancin organai da kuma yawon janyewa (transplant tourism) wanda aka ƙirƙira a ƙarƙashin jagorancin *The Transplantation Society* da *The International Society of Nephrology* a watan Mayu, shekarar 2008.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Transplantation/Janyewa (Transplant):** I've used both the English term "transplantation" and the Hausa term "janyewa (transplant)" to ensure clarity.  "Janyewa" is increasingly used, especially in medical contexts, and including the English term helps with understanding.
*   **Donors/Masu ba da abubuwan ciki:** This translates to "those who give internal organs/materials." It's a descriptive and accurate translation.
*   **Recipients/Mai karɓa:** This translates to "the receiver."
*   **Oversight/Binciken aikin kula da lafiya:** This translates to "the inspection of the work of health authorities."
*   **Trust/Amsar:** This translates to "response/trust."
*   **WHO Guiding Principles:** I've kept the "WHO" acronym as it's widely recognized.  I've translated "Guiding Principles" as *Misakainiyoyin Da Ke Da Daraja* which means "Principles that are of value/importance."
*   **Declaration of Istanbul:** I've kept the "Declaration of Istanbul" as it is a proper name.
*   **Organ Trafficking/Kasuwancin organai:** This translates to "the market/trade of organs."
*   **Transplant Tourism/Yawon janyewa (transplant tourism):** I've used both the English term and the Hausa term.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Terminology Consistency:**  In future translations of related documents, it's crucial to maintain consistent terminology. Create a glossary of key terms.
*   **Context is Key:**  Hausa has regional variations.  Consider the target audience and adjust the language accordingly.
*   **Review by Native Speakers:**  Ideally, have a native Hausa speaker (especially one with medical knowledge) review the translation for accuracy and naturalness.
*   **Transliteration:**  I've used a standard Hausa transliteration.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year. All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.reference: Tafiya da lafiya Taƙaitaccen Bayani: Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na iya barazana na haɗari daban-daban ga lafiya, wanda ya danganta da yanayin matafiyin da ita tafiyar. Matafiya na iya haɗuwa da mahimman canje-canje a lokaci guda a cikin ladabi da yanayin zafi da ƙwayoyin halitta da zafin jiki waɗanda zasu iya haifar da rashin lafiya. Bugu da ƙari, munanan haɗarurruka ga lafiya na iya fitowa a cikin wuraren da babu wadata da ingancin tsaftar jiki da muhalli, ayyukan likitanci ba a tsara su da kyau ba haka kuma babu tsaftataccen ruwa. Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na ƙaruwa, yadda ya zama miliyoyin mutane ke tafiya don aiki, zamantakewa, nishaɗi da kuma dalilai na aikin jin ƙaidalilai kowace shekara. Dole ne dukkanin matafiya su shirya don nau'ikan haɗari ga lafiya da za a iya samu cikin yanayin da ba a sani ba a kafin da yayin da kuma bayan tafiya. Ya kamata mutane da ke shirin tafiya su nemi shawara kan haɗarin da za su iya samu a wurin da zasu je kuma su fahimci hanyar da zasu kare lafiyarsu da kuma yadda zasu rage haɗarin kamuwa da cuta. Shirin gaba sune, matakan da suka dace da taka tsantsan na iya kare lafiyar matafiya, kuma suna rage haɗarin haɗari da samun cuta. Kodayake masana'antar likitanci da masana'antar tafiye-tafiye na iya samar da taimako mai yawa da kuma shawara kyau, ya kasance da alhakin matafiya ne neman bayanai da fahimtar haɗarurrukan da ke ƙunshe da kuma ɗaukan matakan kariya da suka dace don kiyaye lafiyarsu yayin tafiya. Haɗari: Yayin tantance haɗarurrukan lafiya na tafiya ƙasashen a duniya, ya kamata matafiya da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya suyi la'akari da waɗannan manyan abubuwan da za a kawo:
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English document into Hausa, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a general audience. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices and highlight potential nuances.  I'll also provide a transliteration to help with pronunciation.

**Hausa Translation:**

**Bayanin Tafiya da Lafiya**

Kwamancen tafiya zuwa kasashen waje na iya haifar da kasala da dama ga lafiya, dangana da halin mutum da ke tafiya da kuma wurin da yake zuwa.  Matafiya na iya fuskantar canje-canje masu sauri da kuma babban canji a tsayin dangi, zafarkin iska, ƙwayoyin cuta, da kuma zafin jiki, waɗanda ke haifar da rashin lafiya.  Bugu da kari, haɗarin lafiya mai tsanani na iya tasowa a yammaci inda akwai gine-gine mai rauni, tsafta da tsarkakiyar dangi ba su da kyau, ayyukan likita ba su da ƙarfi, kuma ruwan tsarkakiyar dangi ba ya samuwa.  Tafiya zuwa kasashen waje na ƙara yawa, a kowace shekara miliyoyin mutane suna tafiya don dalilai na aiki, zamantauwa, nishaɗi, da kuma taimakon bil'adam.  Dukkan matafiya dole ne su shirya don ir-iriyar haɗarin lafiya da za su fuskanta a wurare da ba su sani ba kafin, a lokacin, da kuma bayan tafiya.  Mutanen da ke shirin tafiya dole ne su nemi shawara kan haɗayoyin da ke cikin wurin da suka zaba kuma su fahimci yadda za su kare lafiyarsu da rage yuwuwar kamuwa da cuta.  Taftalawa daidai, matakan hana cuta da suka dace, da kulawa mai kyau na iya kare lafiyawar matafiya, da rage haɗarin hadura da kamuwa da cuta.  Kodayke jami'an likita da masana'antar tafiya na iya ba da taimako mai yawa da shawara mai kyau, har yanzu ya zama alhakin matafiya neman bayani, fahimtar haɗayoyin da ke cikin tafiya, da kuma ɗaukar matakan da suka dace don kare lafiyarsu yayin tafiya.  Haɗari: Lokacin ƙididdigar haɗarin lafiya na tafiya zuwa kasashen waje, matafiya da ƙwararrun lafiya dole ne su la'aki da abubuwan da ke gaba:

**Transliteration (approximate):**

*Bayanin Tafiya da Lafiya*

*Kwamancen tafiya zuwa kasashen waje na iya haifar da kasala da dama ga lafiya, dangana da halin mutum da ke tafiya da kuma wurin da yake zuwa. Matafiya na iya fuskantar canje-canje masu sauri da kuma babban canji a tsayin dangi, zafarkin iska, ƙwayoyin cuta, da kuma zafin jiki, waɗanda ke haifar da rashin lafiya. Bugu da kari, haɗarin lafiya mai tsanani na iya tasowa a yammaci inda akwai gine-gine mai rauni, tsafta da tsarkakiyar dangi ba su da kyau, ayyukan likita ba su da ƙarfi, kuma ruwan tsarkakiyar dangi ba ya samuwa. Tafiya zuwa kasashen waje na ƙara yawa, a kowace shekara miliyoyin mutane suna tafiya don dalilai na aiki, zamantauwa, nishaɗi, da kuma taimakon bil'adam. Dukkan matafiya dole ne su shirya don ir-iriyar haɗarin lafiya da za su fuskanta a wurare da ba su sani ba kafin, a lokacin, da kuma bayan tafiya. Mutanen da ke shirin tafiya dole ne su nemi shawara kan haɗayoyin da ke cikin wurin da suka zaba kuma su fahimci yadda za su kare lafiyarsu da rage yuwuwar kamuwa da cuta. Taftalawa daidai, matakan hana cuta da suka dace, da kulawa mai kyau na iya kare lafiyawar matafiya, da rage haɗarin hadura da kamuwa da cuta. Kodayke jami'an likita da masana'antar tafiya na iya ba da taimako mai yawa da shawara mai kyau, har yanzu ya zama alhakin matafiya neman bayani, fahimtar haɗayoyin da ke cikin tafiya, da kuma ɗaukar matakan da suka dace don kare lafiyarsu yayin tafiya. Haɗari: Lokacin ƙididdigar haɗarin lafiya na tafiya zuwa kasashen waje, matafiya da ƙwararrun lafiya dole ne su la'aki da abubuwan da ke gaba:*

**Explanation of Choices and Nuances:**

*   **"Bayanin Tafiya da Lafiya"**:  This translates to "Information about Travel and Health" or "Overview of Travel and Health." It's a standard way to introduce a document like this.
*   **"Kwamancen tafiya..."**: "Kwamancen" is used to mean "the act of traveling" or "international travel."
*   **"ƙwayoyin cuta"**: This translates to "microbes" or "disease-causing agents."
*   **"Yammaci"**: This translates to "accommodation" or "lodging."
*   **"Tsafta da tsarkakiyar dangi"**: This is a combined phrase to convey "hygiene and sanitation."
*   **"Ayyukan likita ba su da ƙarfi"**: This means "medical services are not well developed."
*   **"Taftalawa daidai"**: This translates to "Forward planning" or "Proper planning."
*   **"Masana'antar tafiya"**: This translates to "the travel industry."
*   **"la'aki da abubuwan da ke gaba"**: This translates to "consider the following factors."

**Important Considerations for Hausa Translation:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific audience.  Is this for general public consumption, or for healthcare professionals?
*   **Regional Variations:** Hausa has regional dialects. This translation aims for a more standard form, but some words might be preferred in certain areas.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  While this document is fairly neutral, always be mindful of cultural norms when translating health information.

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a low-resource language context. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices and highlight potential areas for further refinement if more context is available.

**Hausa Translation:**

**Hanyar tafiya, wuraren da za su je, lokaci da kwanaki na tafiya, dalilin tafiya, matakin dakin zuwa, tsabtawar abinci da tsafta, yadda mai tafiya yake/take, lafiyar mai tafiya.  Mai tafiya na iya fuskantar kasala na lafiya daban-daban, dangana da inda yake/take zuwa:**  *cizon dabbobi da kwari, gami da kwayar cuta, ticks, fleas, da wasu ruwan gizo, waɗanda ke iya yaduwa cututtuka irin su malaria,  kuma yana iya shiga jini ko sauran ruwan jiki.*

**Transliteration (for easier reading/pronunciation):**

Hanyar tafiya, wuraren da za su je, lokaci da kwanaki na tafiya, dalilin tafiya, matakin dakin zuwa, tsabtawar abinci da tsafta, yadda mai tafiya yake/take, lafiyar mai tafiya. Mai tafiya na iya fuskantar kasala na lafiya daban-daban, dangana da inda yake/take zuwa: cizon dabbobi da kwari, gami da kwayar cuta, ticks, fleas, da wasu ruwan gizo, waɗanda ke iya yaduwa cututtuka irin su malaria, kuma yana iya shiga jini ko sauran ruwan jiki.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **"Mode of transport" - "Hanyar tafiya"**: This is a general term for the way someone travels.
*   **"Destination(s)" - "Wuraren da za su je"**:  Literally "places they will go."  It's a straightforward translation.
*   **"Duration and season of travel" - "Lokaci da kwanaki na tafiya"**:  "Lokaci" means time, and "kwanaki" means days.  Combining them covers both duration and the time of year.
*   **"Purpose of travel" - "Dalilin tafiya"**:  "Dalili" means reason or purpose.
*   **"Standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation" - "Matakin dakin zuwa, tsabtawar abinci da tsafta"**:  "Matakin dakin zuwa" refers to the standard of the accommodation. "Tsabtawar abinci da tsafta" covers food hygiene and sanitation.
*   **"Behaviour of the traveller" - "Yadda mai tafiya yake/take"**:  "Yadda" means "how" or "the way."  "yake/take" is used to indicate that the traveler could be male or female.
*   **"Underlying health of the traveller" - "Lafiyar mai tafiya"**:  "Lafiya" means health.
*   **"Travellers may encounter the following health risks" - "Mai tafiya na iya fuskantar kasala na lafiya daban-daban"**: "Fuskantar" means to encounter or face. "Kasala na lafiya" means health risks.
*   **"animal and insect bites" - "cizon dabbobi da kwari"**: "Cizon" means bite. "Dabbobi" means animals and "kwari" means insects.
*   **"mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies"**: I've kept the English terms "ticks" and "fleas" as they are not commonly used in Hausa and translating them would be difficult without losing accuracy. "Kwayar cuta" is used for mosquitoes. "Ruwan gizo" is used for flies.
*   **"which can spread diseases such as malaria" - "waɗanda ke iya yaduwa cututtuka irin su malaria"**: "Yaduwa" means to spread. "Cututtuka" means diseases.
*   **"exposure to blood or other bodily fluids" - "kuma yana iya shiga jini ko sauran ruwan jiki"**: "Shiga" means to enter or be exposed to. "Jini" means blood. "Ruwan jiki" means bodily fluids.

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:** This translation is based solely on the provided text.  Knowing the *purpose* of this document (e.g., travel advisory, health information leaflet) would allow for more precise and culturally appropriate wording.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For a larger document, it's crucial to establish a glossary of terms to ensure consistency in translation.
*   **Community Review:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by native Hausa speakers, especially those familiar with health and travel terminology.
*   **Loanwords:** I've used some loanwords (like "ticks" and "fleas") because direct Hausa equivalents are either non-existent or would be too obscure. This is a common practice in low-resource language translation.
*   **Gender Neutrality:** I've used "yake/take" to indicate both male and female travelers.

translations.source: environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, temperature foodborne and waterborne diseases inadequate medical services injuries, including from road traffic accidents or recreational activities psychological health, including stress and culture shock Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.reference: Canjin yanayi kamar tsauni, zafi, yanayin zafi/sanyi cutukan da ake samu a abinci da ruwa ƙarancin ayyukan kiwon lafiya raunuka, gami da wanda ake samu daga haɗarin kan titi ko daga ayyukan nishaɗi lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa, gami da damuwa da kuma firgici Hakanan matafiya zasu iya haɗuwa da cututtuka da dama, kuma da yawa daga cikin waɗannan za a iya daƙile su ta hanyar alurar rigakafi. Duk da haka, akwai wasu cututtuka, ciki har da wasu daga cikin mafiya haɗari, waɗanda ba rigakafinsu. Wuraren da inda masauƙi ya ke da tsafta da aikin kiwon lafiya da ruwa mai kyau na da ƙarancin barazana ga lafiyar matafiya. Inda ba a samar da waɗannan ayyukan ba, ya kamata matafiya su ɗauki tsattsauran matakan kariya don gujewa rashin lafiya. Ya kamata a dauƙi gargaɗi bisa tafiya daga majiyoyin gwamnati da mahimmanci.

**Hausa Translation:**

Canjin yanayi, kamar haura kasa, zafin kumshi, da tsanaki, cutan abinci da ruwa, rashin hidima akan lafiya, munananawa, har da hatsarin motoci da wasanni, lafiyar ilimin hankali, gami da damuwa da rudewa da al'ada.  Muna yawan fuskantar cutar da ke yaduwa ta kowane iri, kuma kusan dukkanin cutar za'a hana ta ta hanyar alluran rigakafi.  Amma akwai wasu cutan da ba a samu allura don hana su ba, har da wasu mafi hatsarin.  Yin tafiya zuwa wurare da ke da hanyoyin zama, tsafta, hidimar lafiya, da ingancin ruwa mai kyau, yana da wata matsala kadan ga lafiyar matafiya.  A wurare da ba a samar da waɗannan hidimomi ba, matafiya dole ne su ɗauki matakan tsari don hana cuta.  Ya kamata a ɗauki bayanan gargadi daga hukumomin gwamnati da mutukar jiddau.

*   **Amma akwai wasu cutan da ba a samu allura don hana su ba:**  Am-ma a-kwai wa-su cu-tan da ba a sa-mu al-lu-ra don ha-na su ba

*   **Ya kamata a ɗauki bayanan gargadi daga hukumomin gwamnati da mutukar jiddau:**  Ya ka-ma-ta a dau-ki ba-ya-na-n gar-ga-di da-ga hu-ku-mo-min gwam-na-ti da mu-tu-kar ji-ddau

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Formal vs. Informal:** I've aimed for a relatively formal tone, suitable for health advisories and travel information.  Hausa has different registers, and a more informal translation would be possible.
*   **"Foodborne and waterborne diseases":** Translated as "cutan abinci da ruwa" (diseases of food and water). This is a common and understandable way to express this concept.

*   **"Governmental sources":** Translated as "hukumomin gwamnati" (authorities of the government).
*   **"Take seriously":** Translated as "ɗauki da mutukar jiddau" (take with seriousness).

**Important Considerations for Hausa Translation:**

translations.source: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up; obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.reference: Yin rigakafi: Kafin tafiya: tuntuɓi cibiyar magungunan matafiya ko likita, mafi dacewa, makonni 4-8 kafin tafiya don karɓar duk wani rigakafi da ake buƙata; Yi karatu kan cututtukan gida masu alaƙa da wurin da zaka je; nemi bayani game da haɗarin zazzabin cizon sauro da rigakafin cizon sauro. translations.source: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB). Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.reference: Tarin fuka Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ƙwayar cuta mai suna Mycobacterium tuberculosis ce ke haifar da cutar tarin fuka, kuma mafi yawan lokaci tana shafar huhu. Tarin fuka na yaɗuwa ta hanyar iska idan mutane masu ƙwayar cutar a huhunsu suka yi tar, attishawa ko tofar da yawu. Mutum na buƙatar shaƙar ƙwayoyin cutar kaɗan ne kawai kafin ya kamu da ita. A kowace shekara, mutane miliyan 10 ne ke kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka(TB). Duk da kasancewarta cuta da za a iya karewa kuma ana iya warkewa, mutane miliyan 1.5 suna mutuwa daga tarin fuka a kowace shekara - wanda ya sa ta zama babbar cuta mai yaɗuwa da ke kisa a duniya. Tarin fuka na kan gaba a jerin sanadin mutuwar mutane masu cuta mai karyar garkuwar jiki HIV, kuma yana samar da kaso mai yawa na cutuka masu ƙin jin magani Yawancin masu kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka na rayuwa a ƙasashe masu ƙaranci da matsakaicin ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, amma ana samun TB a ko'ina a duniya. Ana iya samun kusan rabin dukkan masu fama da tarin fuka a kasashe 8: Bangladesh, China, Indiya, Indonesiya, Najeriya, Pakistan, Philippines da Afirka ta Kudu. An ƙiyasta cewa kimanin kaso ɗaya bisa huɗu na al'ummar duniya na kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar TB, sai dai yawancin mutane ba za su kama cutar ta tarin fuka ba, wasu kuma jikinsu na yaƙar cutar.

**Hausa Translation:**

Ciwon TB yana yaduwa ta iska lokacin mutane da ke da ciwon koda a makogwaronsu suna ƙara wuya, ƙatsina ko fitar fitsari. Mutum ya sha kusan ƴan kwayoyin cuta ne a cikin iska don ya kamu da cuta. Kowane shekara, mutane miliyan 10 suna jikkata da ciwon koda (TB). Albeit an hana shi da kuma janyewa, mutane miliyan 1.5 suna mutuwa ta amsar ciwon TB kowace shekara – ya zama kisan cuta mafi yawan duniya. Ciwon TB shine babban abin da ke haifar da mutuwar mutanen da ke da HIV, kuma babban abin da ke kawo matsalar jinin antimicrobial.  Mutane da yawa da ke jikkata da ciwon TB suna zaune a kasashe da ke da matsakaicin kudin shiga da kuma matalala, amma ciwon TB yana kasancewa a ko'in duniya. Kusan rabin mutanen da ke da ciwon TB za su taso a kasashe 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines da South Africa. Kusan kwatar mutanen duniya an kiyasta cewa sun kamu da kwayoyin cutar TB, amma yawancin mutane ba za su haɓaka cutar TB ba, kuma wasu za su ceto cutar.

translations.source: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others. If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.reference: Waɗanda ke ɗauke da ƙwayar cutar amma ba su kamu da rashin lafiya ba tukunna ba sa iya yaɗa ta. Mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka na da hatsarin tsawon rayuwa na kaso 5 zuwa 10 na kamuwa da cutar ta TB. Mutanen da ke da raunin garkuwar jiki, kamar mutanen da ke rayuwa da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki wato HIV, ƙaranci abinci mai gina jiki ko ciwon suga, ko masu shan sigari, sun fi zama cikin hatsarin yin rashin lafiyar. Alamomi: Alamomin cutar tarin fuka sun haɗa da: Taro na lokaci mai tsawo, Ciwon ƙirji, Kasala ko gajiya, Ramewa, Zazzaɓi, Gumin dare. Mafi yawan lokuta, waɗannan alamomi sukan kasance marasa tsanani na tsawon watanni, wanda hakan ke haifar da jinkiri wajen neman lafiya da kuma ƙara hatsarin yaɗa cutar ga wasu. Idan ma'aikacin lafiya ya yi zargin mara lafiya na fama a cutar tarin fuka, zai aika wannan marar lafiyar domin yin gwaji. Ta ɓangaren cutar tarin fuka da ta shafi huhu, za a buƙaci mara lafiya da ya ba da yawunsa don a yi gwajin cutar ta tarin fuka. Ga tarin fuka da bai shafi huhu ba kuma, ana iya gwada samfurin ruwan jiki ko ko wani ɓangare da cutar ta shafa. WHO na ba da shawara yin gwajin gano ƙwayoyin cuta cikin sauri a matsayin gwajin farko ga mutanen da ke nuna alamun kamuwa da tarin fuka. Sauran hanyoyin gwaje-gwajen na iya haɗawa da duba yawu a ƙarƙashin madubin binciken ƙwaƙwaf da kuma hoton ƙirji.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a low-resource language context. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices and highlight potential areas for further refinement if more context is available.

**Hausa Translation:**

Mutane da aka kamu da cutar, amma ba su da ciwon (haka nan) ba, ba za su iya yaduwa da ita ba. Mutanen da aka kamu da kwayoyin cutar TB suna da babban haddi na 5-10% na samun ciwo a rayuwarsu. Mutanen da ke da tsarin jinin da ya lalace, kamar mutanen da ke rayuwa da HIV, rashin abinci, ko sukari, ko kuma mutanen da suke amfani da tabawa, suna da babban haddi na samun ciwo. Alamomin: Alamomin cutar TB sun haɗa da: Tsarin kasala mai tsawon lokaci, Ciwon kuki, Rashin ƙarfi ko gajiya, Ƙarancin jima, Zafin jiki, Ƙanƙara daci. Sau da yawa, waɗannan alama za su zama ƙanƙantar a cikin watanni da yawa, don haka hakan ya haɗa ƙalubalantar neman kula da lafiya da ƙara haɗarin yaduwar cutar ga wasu. Idan mai ba da aikin lafiya ya yi tunanin mai haƙuri yana da cutar TB, za a aika mai haƙuri don gwaje-gwaje. A cikin yanayin cutar TB na huhu, za a bukaci marasa lafiya su ba alayewar guba don gwaje-gwaje don kwayoyin cutar TB. Don cutar TB da ba ta cikin huhu, alayewar jinin jiki da kuma kayan aikin jiki za a gwaje. WHO ta ƙara waɗancan gwaje-gwaje na juzu'i na sauri a matsayin gwaje-gwaje na farko ga mutanen da ke nuni da alama da alama na TB. translations.source: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable. It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.reference: Wajen kamuwa da tarin fuka, mutum na gamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka wacce ta zama a jiki ba tare da wani tasiri ba. Kamuwa da wannan ƙwayar cutar na iya juyewa ya zama cikakkiyar cutar tarin fuka idan garkuwar jikinsu ta samu rauni. Ta yiwu mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka ba za su nuna wasu alamomin cutar ta tarin fuka ba. Don gano ko an kamu da cutar tarin fuka, ma'aikatan lafiya za su duba marasa lafiya da ke cikin haɗari don kawar da yiwuwar kamuwa da tarin fuka, kuma suna iya amfani da gwajin fata ko jini don bincikar tarin fuka. Magani: Tarin fuka cuta ce da ake iya warkewa. Ana magance ta ta hanyar shan daidaitattun magunguna kala 4 na tsawon wata 6. Magungunan da ake yawan amfani da su sun haɗa da rifampicin da isoniazid. Amma wasu lokutan cutar tarin fuka ba ta karɓar daidaitattun magungunan. A wannan yanayin, mara lafiyan yana fama da tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani. Tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani na ɗaukar lokaci mai tsawo kuma yana da sarƙaƙiya.

**Hausa Translation:**

Tare da cutar TB, mutum ya kamu da kwayoyin cutar TB waɗanda ke zama mara ƙarfi a jiki. Wannan kamuwa na iya haɓaka zuwa cuta ta TB idan tsarin jikin mutumin ya waci. Mutanen da ke da kamuwa da cutar TB ba sa nuna alama ko alama mai illa ta TB. Don gano kamuwa da cutar TB, masu aiki da lafiya za su bincika mutanen da ke cikin haɗari don kawar da cutar TB mai aiki, kuma suna iya amfani da gwajin fata ko jini don duba kamuwa da cutar TB. Magani: Cutar TB na iya jujjuyawa. Ana maganinta da kuma amfani da abubuwan da ke hana cuta na watanni 6 guda, gami da abubuwan da ke hana cuta guda huɗu. Misalan kwayoyin da ake amfani da su akwai rifampisin da isoniazid. A wasu lokuta, kwayoyin cutar TB ba sa amsa ga kwayoyin da ke hana cuta na yau da kullun. A wannan yanayin, mai haɗari yana da cutar TB mai jure abubuwan hana cuta. Maganin cutar TB mai jure abubuwan hana cuta ya fi tsayi kuma ya fi tsanani.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **TB:** I've kept "TB" as is, as it's commonly used and understood, even in Hausa.  Using the full Hausa translation of "Tuberculosis" would be too cumbersome.
*   **"Inactive" bacteria:** I translated "lie inactive" as "mara ƙarfi" (weak/not strong). This conveys the idea of the bacteria being dormant.
*   **"Weakens" (immune system):** I used "ya waci" which means "to weaken" or "to become feeble."
*   **"At-risk patients":** I translated this as "mutanen da ke cikin haɗari" (people who are in danger/at risk).
*   **"Rule out active TB":** I used "don kawar da cutar TB mai aiki" (to eliminate active TB).
*   **"Curable":** I used "na iya jujjuyawa" (can be cured/healed).
*   **"Standard 6-month course":** I translated this as "abubuwan da ke hana cuta na watanni 6 guda" (medications for prevention for 6 months).
*   **"Drug-resistant TB":** I translated this as "cutar TB mai jure abubuwan hana cuta" (TB that endures/tolerates medications).
*   **"Longer and more complex":** I used "ya fi tsayi kuma ya fi tsanani" (is longer and more serious/complex).

translations.source: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time. Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.reference: Ana ba wa marar lafiyar maganin tarin fuka da yake buƙata tare da bayanai da kuma sa'ido da tallafi daga ma'aikacin lafiya ko wani mai aikin sa kai da aka horas. Idan babu tallafi, tabbatar da an kammala shan maganin na iya zama abu mai wahala. Idan ba a kammala shan maganin yadda ya kamata ba, cutar na iya juyewa zuwa wacce ba ta jin magani kuma ta yaɗu. Game da kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka kuwa (ta yadda mara lafiyar ya kamu da ƙwayar cutar amma bai fara rashin lafiya ba), ana iya ba shi maganin kariya daga cutar tarin fuka don hana bayyanar alamomin cutar. Maganin iri ɗaya ake sha da na magance cutar, amma na lokacin da bai kai wancan tsawo ba. Zaɓuɓɓukan magani na baya-bayan nan sun ƙara gajarce tsawon lokacin shan maganin zuwa wata 1 ko 3 kawai, idan aka kwatanta da watanni 6 da akan yi a baya.

translations.source: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide. UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.reference: Hasken Ultraviolet radiation Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) na nufin hasken da ya faɗo a ma'aunin haske na 100-400nm, wanda ya fi ƙarfi kuma yake da ƙarancin nisan zango fiye da da hasken da ake iya gani da ido. UV radiation na samuwa ne daga rana, amma kuma ana iya ƙirƙirarsa ta hanyoyin da ɗan adam ya samar waɗanda ake amfani da su a masana'antu, kasuwanci da motsa jiki. Yankin UV ya ƙunshi ma'aunin haske 100-400 nm kuma an raba shi zuwa rukuni uku: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm) UVC (100-280 nm). Yayin da hasken rana ke wuce sararin samaniya, shimfiɗar sama, tururin ruwa, iskar oxygen da ta carbon dioxide na shanye duk hasken da ke rukunin UVC da kuma kaso 90% na wanda ke rukunin UVB. Hasken rukunin UVA bai cika tasirantuwa da sinadaran da ke sararin samaniya ba. Dun haka, hasken UV da ke isowa duniya yawanci ya ƙunshi UVA ne da kaɗan daga UVB. Adadin hasken UV radiation daga rana da ke isowa duniya ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, ciki har da tsayin rana a sararin sama, nisa ta ɓangaren latitude, kaurin gajimare, nisan altitude, kaurin shimfiɗar sararin samaniya ta ozone da yanayin ƙasa. Rage kauri da shimfiɗar sararin samaniya ta ozone ta yi saboda gurɓacewar yanayi da ɗan adam ya haifar yana ƙara adadin UVA da UVB waɗanda ke isowa duniya. Wannan na iya yin tasiri ga lafiyar ɗan adam, dabbobi, halittun ruwa da rayuwar shuka.

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

**Tafsirar fitar da ƙarfi mai launin rawaya (Ultraviolet):** Fitar da ƙarfi mai launin rawaya (UV) ta ƙunshi juyin wayaye daga 100 zuwa 400 nm, wanda ke da babban ƙarfi da ƙanƙantar da wayar da ido. Fitar da UV ta zo ta hanyar yawan gaske daga rana, amma kuma za'a iya samar da ita ta hanyar abubuwan da mutane suka ƙirƙira waɗanda ake amfani da su a masana'antu, kasuwanci da nishaɗi.  Yayin da rana ke haska, ta hanyar iska, duk fitar da UVC da kusan 90% na fitar da UVB an shafe su ne ta hanyar ozone, ruwan iska, iska da carbon dioxide. Fitar da UVA ba ta shafar iska sosai. Don haka, fitar da UV da ke kaiwa kasashen duniya ta kasance babban bangare na UVA tare da ɗan bangare na UVB. Adadin fitar da UV daga rana da ke taba kasashen duniya ya dogara ne akan abubuwan da dama, gami da tsayin rana a sararin sama, latitud, ruwan sama, hazaƙa, ƙwarin lafiyar ozone da maye gurbin ƙasa. Ragowar lafiyar ozone ta amsar cutarwa na mutane tana ƙara adadin UVA da UVB da ke kaiwa kasashen duniya. Wannan na iya shafar lafiya ta mutane, dabbobi, ƙungiyoyin ruwa da rayuwar tsire-tsire.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Ultraviolet Radiation"**: I translated this as "Fitar da ƙarfi mai launin rawaya (UV)".  Keeping the abbreviation "UV" in parentheses is common practice in Hausa when introducing technical terms.

*   **Technical Terms:** I tried to use the most accurate and commonly understood Hausa terms for scientific concepts.  For example, "ground reflection" became "maye gurbin ƙasa."
*   **Sentence Structure:** I adjusted sentence structure slightly to flow more naturally in Hausa.  Hausa often prefers a slightly different word order than English.
*   **Clarity:** I prioritized clarity and readability for a general audience.
*   **Ozone Layer:** "Lafiyar ozone" is the standard translation for "ozone layer."
*   **Human-created pollution:** "cutarwa na mutane" is used to convey the meaning of human-created pollution.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.

translations.source: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck. Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.reference: A cikin mutane, ƙari a haske UV na iya haifar da ciwon sankarar fata, amosanin ido da karya garkuwar jiki. Kariya: ƙari a yawan masu kamuwa da cutar kansar fata a cikin gomman shekarun da suka gabata yana da alaƙa mai ƙarfi da ƙarin shiga rana yayin ayyukan waje da kuma hanyoyin samar da hasken UV da ɗan adam ya samar kamar fitilu masu haske kamar rana da gadajen sauya launin fata. Har ila yau, amfani da ya wuce gona da iri shi ne sanadin cutar da idanu da gaskuwar jiki. Ɗaukar wasu 'yan matakan na kiyayewa na iya rage hatsarin kamuwa da illolin na lafiya: Iyakance lokaci shiga tsakar rana daga 10 na safe zuwa 4 na yamma, musamman a ranakun da ma'aunin UV ya yi sama Lokacin da hasken UV ya fi tsanani kuma dole sai ka fita waje, yi ƙoƙari ku nemo gilashin kare rana kuma ka sanya tufafin kariya, gami da hula mai malafa da za ta ba da kariya daga rana ga kai, fuska da wuyanka. Yi amfani da man shiga rana na aƙalla ajin 15 SPF - ko wanda ya fi shi - kuma a sake shafa bayan duk awa 2. Gujewa amfani da fitilu masu haske irin na rana da wuraren sauya launin fata, domin an san suna lalata fata da idanu. A taƙaita shigar yara hasken UV saboda suna wani mataki mai muhimmanci na girma don haka sun fi manya saurin fuskantar matsala daga ababe masu hatsari a muhalli. Gaɓoɓi masu muhimmanci, kamar garkuwar jikin ɗan adam ba su kammalu ba yayin da ake haihuwar mutum, don haka muhalli mara aminci na iya kawo cikas a ci gabansu kamar yadda ya dace. Martani daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Hukumar na aiki da ƙasashen da ke ƙarƙashinta da kuma abokan hulɗarta don ƙara wayar da kai game da hatsarin da ke tattare da yawan ta'ammali da haske ultraviolet radiation.

translations.source: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.reference: Shirin INTERSUN shiri ne na haɗin gwiwa tsakanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Shirin Muhalli na Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya, Ƙungiyar Lura da Sararin Samaniya ta World Meteorological Organization, cibiyar binciken da ya shafi cutar sankara ta International Agency on Cancer Research da kuma hukumar International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. Yana ƙarfafawa da tantance bincike a kan UV radiation ga lafiya, kuma yana samar da hanyoyin agaji da suka dace ta hanyar samar da jagora, shawarwari da yaɗa bayanai. Manufar shirin ta haɗa da bayar da shawarwari masu inganci kan illar da hasken UV ke da shi ga lafiya da muhalli, karfafa gwiwar ƙasashe su ɗauki matakin rage illa ga lafiya da UV ke haifarwa da kuma samar da tsari ga mahukuntan ƙasa game da shirye-shiryen wayar da kan hasken rana. Don cimma wannan manufar, shirin yana haɗa gwiwa da masana da ƙwararrun hukumomi wajen aiwatar da muhimman ayyukan bincike, ganowa da ƙididdige haɗarin UV radiation ga lafiya, samar da ingantaccen hasashe game da illar da ke tattare da canji a yawan ta'ammali da hasken UV ga lafiya da muhalli, da lalata sararin samaniya, da samar da hanyoyin da za a iya amfani da su wajen saka ido kan canje-canje masu tasiri kan lafiya da hasken UV ke haifarwa bayan tsawon lokaci. Shirin yana samar da kayan aiki da shawarwari da suka danganci ma'aunin UV, kariya daga hasken rana ga yara, gadaje masu fitar da haske irin na rana, yawon shaƙatawa da tabbatar da lafiya yayin gudanar aiki ko sana'a.

translations.source: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there. Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.reference: Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a faɗin duniya wato Universal Health Coverage Taƙaitaccen bayani: Universal Health Coverage (UHC) wato samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a faɗin duniya na nufin cewa kowanne mutum na da damar amfani da cikakku kuma ingantattun ayyukan lafiya da suke buƙata, a lokacin da suke buƙata kuma a wajen da suke buƙatarsu, ba tare da matsalar rashin kuɗi ba. Ya haɗa da cikakkun ayyukan lafiya na tilas, daga wayar da kai zuwa kariya, magance cuta, gyaran hali da kula da masu matsanantan cututtuka. Domin cika wannan alƙawarin, akwai buƙatar ƙasashe su kasance suna da tsarin kiwon lafiya da yake da ƙarfi, yake aiki kuma suke aiki ga kowa ba tare da wariya ba, masu tushe a cikin al'ummar da suke yi wa aiki. Kula da lafiya matakin farko ne hanya mafi aiki kuma mai arha wajen samar da wannan tsari na kiwon lafiya. Kowace ƙasa na da hanya ta daban ta cimma gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya su kuma yanke shawarar abin da za su yi ta la'akari da buƙatun al'ummarsu da kuma kayan aikin da suke da su. Sanya jari a Matakin Farko na Kula da Lafiya na tabbatar da cewa an gane dukkan waɗannan buƙatu, an ba su muhimmanci kuma an warware su ta hanyar da ta haɗa da kowane ɓangare; an samar da ma'aikatan lafiya wadatattu masu sanin makamar aiki; kuma dukkan ɓangarori a al'umma suna ba da gudummuwa wajen fuskantar ƙalubalen muhalli da tattalin arziƙi da ke shafar lafiya da walwala, wanda ya haɗa da shirya wa, kai agaji da kuma farfaɗowa da buƙatun lafiya da kan taso cikin gaggawa. Martanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: A matsayin wani tushe na samar da Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya (UHC) Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya na ba da shawarar sake saita tsare-tsaren kula da lafiya ta amfani da tsarin kula da lafiya a matakin farko (PHC). Tabbatar da samuwar gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya (UHC) shiri ne mai muhimmanci na Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta duniya, da burin samun ƙarin mutane biliyan 1 da za su ci moriyar tsarin kula da lafiya na duniya a shekarar 2025. A ƙasashe masu tsarin kula da lafiya mai rauni, muna mayar da hankali kan samar da taimakon dabarun aiki don gina hukumomi na ƙasa da ayyukan da za su cike muhimman gurabe a lokacin buƙatun gaggawa.

**Hausa Translation:**

**Kwarewar Lafiya Mai Karbuwa Ga Kowa: Bayani**

Kwarewar lafiya mai karbuwa ga kowa (UHC) na nufin duk mutane suna da damar samun dukkanin ayyukan lafiya na ingila da suke bukata, idan ya zo lokacinsa da kuma inda suke bukata, ba tare da rikici ko wahala a kan kudin da za su biya ba.  Yana ruƙo da dukkanin juyin aikin lafiya na da mahimmanci, daga haɓaka lafiya zuwa hana cuta, jinya, gyara hali, da kuma kula da marasa lafiya. Don aiwatar da wannan alkawari, kasashe suna buƙatar da tsarin lafiya na ƙarfi, ingantacciya, da kuma mai daidaito, waɗanda ke ƙarƙashin al'ummomin da suke hidima.  Babban kula da lafiya (PHC) shi ne mafi kyawun hanyar da za a kai ga wannan buri, kuma hakanan mafi arha. Kowane ƙasa yana da hanya daban don cimmawa UHC, da kuma yanke hukunci akan abin da za a rufe bisa ga buƙatun mazauna da kuma albarkatun da ke akwai.  Zuba jari a cikin PHC yana tabbatar da cewa duk waɗannan buƙatun an gano su, an shirya su, kuma an yi amfani da su a hanyar da ta haɗa juna; cewa akwai ma'aikata na lafiya da kula da jinya na ƙarfi da kuma da kayan aiki; da kuma cewa dukkanin bangarorin al'umma suna ba da gudummawa wajen fafatawa da abubuwan da ke haɓaka lafiya da kuma alfanin rayuwa, gami da shirye-shiryen, amsa da kuma farfado da matsala.

**Amma sa ido na WHO:**

A matsayin ginshiƙi don UHC, WHO na ƙara waɗawar canja hankali ga tsarin lafiya ta amfani da hanyar babban kula da lafiya (PHC).  Kasantuwar UHC ita ce babban burin ƙa'ida na WHO, da burin cewa mutane biliyan ɗaya da ƙari suna amfanar da kwarewar lafiya mai karbuwa zuwa shekarar 2025. A cikin kasashe da ke da tsarin lafiya mai rauni, muna mai da hankali ga taimakon fasaha don gina hukumomin kasa da kuma samar da hidima don cikaɓeɓɓayan buƙatar a lokacin gaggawa.

---

*   **"Technical Assistance"**: Translated as "taimakon fasaha" - a standard translation.
*   **"Fragile health systems"**: Translated as "tsarin lafiya mai rauni" - meaning "weak or vulnerable health systems."
*   **Flow and Readability:** I've tried to ensure the Hausa translation flows naturally and is easy to understand for a Hausa-speaking audience. I've used common Hausa phrasing and sentence structures.

translations.source: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously. WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.reference: A wuraren da ke da tsarin kula da lafiya tsayayye, muna ƙarfafar samar da tasirin kula da lafiyar al'umma ta ɓangaren faɗaɗa aikin ga kowa ta hanyar samar da dokiki don samar da tsare-tsaren da za a ci moriya nan gaba da kuma ba da muhimmin tallafi wajen inganta aiki. Wannan aiki ana yinsa bisa bin tsarin aiwatarwa da aka saba da kuma yarjejeniyoyi; bayanai, bincike da ƙirƙira; da shugabanci a fagen diflomasiyya, wayar da kai, daidaiton jinsi, wadatar kula da lafiya da haƙƙoƙin al'umma, ayyukan da suka shafi ɓangarori da dama, da kuma hada-hadar kuɗi. Ajandar 2030 ta aikace-aikacen Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya ta dace da Manufofin Ci gaba Mai Ɗorewa (SDG'S) na 3.8 wanda yake mayar da hankali wajen samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a duniya, wanda ya haɗa da ba da kariya ga marasa ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, damar amfani da ingantattun ayyukan kula da lafiya da kuma damar amfani da magangunan da alluran rigakafi da ake buƙata masu aminci, da suke aiki, ingantattu kuma masu sauƙin saye ga kowa. A duniya bakiɗaya, ci gaba a fannin samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya abu ne da ke fuskantar ƙalubale. Ci gaba ta fannin adadin mutanen da ke amfana da ayyukan lafiya ya samu tsaiko yayin da adadin al'ummar da ke fuskantar mummunan kashe kuɗi don neman lafiya ke ci gaba da ƙaruwa. Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya ta ci gaba da sa ido kan ci gaban tana kuma wallafa rahoto game da halin da duniya ke ciki don bayyana shaida mafi sabunta bayan duk shekara biyu. Duba rahotannin a nan kuma ka ziyarci shafin sahihin bayani game da Gamammen Tsarin Kula da lafiya (UHC) don ƙarin bayani.

Aikinmu yana da tallafin jagorancin ƙa'ida da yarjejeniyoyi; bayanan, bincike da sabbin abubuwan; da jagorancin fannin dIPLOMASI, shawara, daidaito da jima'i, kiyayyar lafiya, haƙƙin ɗan'adam, aikin da ya shafi fannin da dama, da kudi.

Dabarar 2030 don SDGs Aikin WHO ya haɗu da burin SDG na 3.8, wanda ke mai da hankali kan cimma kiyayyar lafiya ta kowa, gami da kiyaye hada mutane daga kashe kudi akan lafiya, samun isasshen aikin lafiya mai kyau, da samun abubuwan da suka dace, ingantattun, da kuma kasanya kudin da za'a biya akan abubuwan lafiya da allurai. A duniya, ci gaba da kiyayyar lafiya ta kowa yana da ƙalubale. Ci gaba a cikin hidima ya tsaya a wuri guda yayin da yawan mutane da ke fuskantar kashe kudi akan lafiya ya ƙara ne da ƙarfi. WHO tana lura da ci gaba kuma tana fitar da rahotannin duniya don bayyana sababbin bayanannai duk shekarar biyu.  Kalli rahotannin nan da ziyarci shafin gaskiya game da UHC don ƙarin bayani.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Robust health system settings"**: Translated as "A cikin tsarin lafiya na ƙarfi" (In a strong/robust health system).
*   **"Policy dialogue"**: Translated as "tattaunawar manufa" (policy discussion/dialogue).
*   **"Strategic support to improve performance"**: Translated as "tallafi mai ƙarfi don ƙirƙirar ƙwarewar aiki" (strong support to create work performance).
*   **"Normative guidance and agreements"**: Translated as "jagorancin ƙa'ida da yarjejeniyoyi" (guidance of rules and agreements).
*   **"Multisectoral action"**: Translated as "aiki da ya shafi fannin da dama" (action that affects many fields/sectors).
*   **"2030 Agenda for SDGs"**:  Kept as "Dabarar 2030 don SDGs" (2030 Plan for SDGs).  Using the acronym "SDGs" is common even in Hausa when discussing international development.
*   **"Universal Health Coverage (UHC)"**: Translated as "kiyayyar lafiya ta kowa" (health coverage for everyone).  I've also included "UHC" in parentheses after the first mention for clarity.
*   **"Catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending"**: Translated as "kashe kudi akan lafiya" (spending money on health).
*   **"Fact sheet"**: Translated as "shafin gaskiya" (page of facts).
*   **Diplomacy**: Translated as "dIPLOMASI" (Diplomacy).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Hausa has a rich vocabulary, but sometimes direct translations don't capture the nuance of technical terms. I've tried to prioritize clarity and accuracy within the context of public health.
*   **Acronyms:**  International acronyms are often used directly in Hausa, especially in formal documents.
*   **Readability:** I've aimed for a translation that is easily understandable to a broad audience.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with expertise in public health to ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness.

translations.source: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset. However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.reference: Kula da lafiya a birane Taƙaitaccen bayani: Faɗaɗar birani na ɗaya daga cikin ababen na kan gaba da ke tashe a duniya a ƙarni na 21 wanda ke da tasiri sosai a kan lafiya. Sama sa kaso 55 da al'ummar duniya na rayuwa a birane - ana sa rai wannan adadin zai ƙaru zuwa kaso 68 nan da 2050. Da yake faɗaɗa birane da zai zo nan gaba zai auku ne a ƙasashe masu tasowa, duniyar a yau tana da wata dama ta musamman ta tsara yadda faɗaɗar biranen za ta kasance da ma sauran manyan ababen da suka shafi ci gaban birane a wata hanya da za ta ba da kariya ta kuma ƙarfafi kiwon lafiya. Wannan yana da muhimmanci, musamman da yake lafiya da walwalar al'umma na iya zama kadara mafi daraja ta kowanne birni. Sai dai, da yawa daga cikin mutane biliyan 4.2 da ke rayuwa a birane na fama da matsalar ƙarancin muhalli da hanyoyin sufuri, rashin tsaftar muhalli da sarrafa shara, da kuma ingancin iskar shaƙa wanda bai cika sharuɗɗan da hukumar lafiya ta duniya ta bayar ba. Sauran nau'ukan gurɓatar yanayi, kamar su hayaniya, ruwa da guɓacewar ƙasa, da wuraren da ake kira tsibiran zafi na birane, da rashin sarari don yin tafiyar ƙafa, tuƙa keke da kuma rayuwa mai cike da kuzari sun haɗu sun sanya biranen zama cibiyar annobar cutukan da ba sa yaɗuwa da kuma haddasa sauyin yanayi. Ma'ajiyar Bayanai ta Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya game da Kiwon Lafiya a Birane Tasiri: Kusan kaso 40 na faɗaɗar birane na faruwa ne a unguwannin talakawa da ba su da ruwa mai tsafta da tsaftar muhalli, kuma kaso 91 na mutanen da ke zaune a birane na shaƙar gurɓatacciyar iska. Idan ana batun abinci mai lafiya, faɗaɗar birane na ƙara nesanta mutane da gonaki wanda ke ƙara ƙarfafar buƙatar abincin da aka sarrafa wanda ba shi da kyau ga jiki.

Sama da kashi 55% na yawan jama'ar duniya suna zaune a birane – kashi da ake sa ran zai tashi zuwa kashi 68% a shekarar 2050.  Tunda yawancin karuwar birane a gaba za ta faru a kasashen da ke tasirinsa, duniya ta samu damar daban a yau don daidaita karuwar birane da sauran manyan abubuwan da ke faruwa a birane, ta hanyar kare lafiya da kuma bunkasuwa.  Wannan ya zama wajibi, musamman saboda lafiyar mata da maza da kuma koshin lafiya na warga shine daya daga mafi muhimman abubuwan da birnin ke da shi.  Amma, yawancin mutane miliyan 4.2 da ke zaune a birane suna fama da gida mara kyau da kuma hanyoyin sufewa, tsafta mai rauni da kuma kula da shararba, da isar da iska mai kyau da ta biyo bayan dokokin WHO. Sauran matakan cutarwa, kamar kasala, ruwan da ya canza launi, da ƙaƙƙarar ƙasa, – wacce ake kiran "tsarin zafi na birane" – da kuma kawar da wurin tafiya, hawan keke da kuma rayuwar da ke da aiki, sun haɗu don sa birane su zama cibiyoyin cutar da ba sa haɗawa da cuta (noncommunicable diseases) da kuma haɓaka canjin yanayi. Lokacin da aka zo ga abinci mai kyau, karuwar yawan jama'a a birane tana daɗaɗa nisa daga gonaki zuwa teku, wanda ke haɓaka buƙatar abinci mai cutarwa, abinci mai aikin gona.

translations.source: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea; noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.reference: Mazauna birane suna cikin hatsarin fuskantar abubuwan da sauyin yanayi ke haifar wa saboda sun dogara da makamashi mai ƙonewa wajen sufuri, girki da ɗumama gidaje. Birane ne ke da alhakin samar fiye da kaso biyu bisa uku na makamashi a duniya kuma suke fitar da kaso 60 na gurɓatacciyar iskar gas, kuma waɗanda ke birane na iya fuskantar yanayin da ya fi na ƙauyukan da ke kewaye da su tsauri da digiri 3-5 saboda yaɗuwar gine-gine da ƙarancin buɗaɗɗun wurare masu bishiyoyi. Dukkan waɗannan na saka mazauna birane cikin hatsari ta fuskar lafiya. Yawancin sabubban mutuwa na kan gaba guda 10 na da alaƙa ta kusa da faɗaɗar birane cikin sauri kuma ba bisa tsari ba, da kuma tsarawa da shirya birane ba yadda ya kamata ba. Biranen suna fuskantar ninki uku na hatsarin yaɗuwar cututtuka kamar HIV/AIDS, tarin fuka, limoniya, zazzaɓin dengue da gudawa; da cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa kamar cututtukan zuciya, bugun jini, asma, kansa, ciwon sukari da damuwa; da kuma tashin hankali da raunuka, ciki har da rauni da hatsarin ababen hawa. Martani daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Duk da ƙalubalen da suke fuskanta, biranen na iya samar da damammaki don ingantacciyar lafiya, tsabtataccen muhalli da ayyukan da suka shafi inganta yanayi. Dole ne dokoki masu ƙarfi na birane su ba da fifiko kan kiwon lafiya, saboda tana da mahimmanci wajen samar da rayuwa mai kyau a birane, samar da ma'aikata masu ƙwazo, samar da al'ummomin masu juriya da karsashi, ba da damar tafiye-tafiye, haɓaka hulɗar zamantakewa da kare al'umma masu rauni. Tsarin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya game da lafiyar birane ya mayar da hankali kan samun ingantacciyar iska, ruwa da muhalli mai tsafta, da sauran abubuwan da ke tabbatar da muhalli; lafiyayyen tsarin birane; muhalli mai cike da ƙoshin lafiya kuma mara hayaƙi; tafiye-tafiye cikin aminci da lafiya; hana aukuwar tashin hankali da raunuka; goyon bayan ingantaccen tsarin samar da abinci; kula da muhalli ta fannin sa ido kan cututtukan da ƙwari masu shan jini ke yaɗawa; da kuma shirya birane wajen fuskantar buƙatun gaggawa.
