translations.source: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.” Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.reference: WHO ta taya Benin da Mali murnar kawar da cutar trachoma a matsayin cuta mai addabar al'umma WHO ta tabbatar da cewa Benin da Mali sun yi nasarar kawar da trachoma a matsayin cuta mai addabar al'umma, wanda ya sa suka zama ƙasashe na biyar da na shida a yankin da WHO ke kula da shi a Afirka da suka kai wannan matakin. Ƙasashen da a baya suka samu wannan tantancewar ta kawar da cutar trachoma su ne Ghana(Yuni 2018), Gambia (Afrilu 2021), Togo (Mayu 2022) da Malawi (Satumba 2022). Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na ya ce hukumar lafiya ta duniyar tana kuma jinjinawa hukumomin lafiya na Benin da Mali, da kuma ƙungiyoyin haɗin guiwa na ƙasashen waje da na gida bisa wannan nasara da aka samu. Biyo bayan nasarar Benin da Mali, har yanzu trachoma cuta ce mai yaɗuwa a ƙasashen 23 a yankin Afirka da WHO ke lura da shi, wanda hakan ya ƙara kusanta mu muradin da kawar da cutar trachoma da aka tsara don lura da cutatuttukan da ake samu a yankuna masu zafi a shekarar 2021 zuwa 2030. A duniya bakiɗaya, Benin da Mali sun bi sahun wasu ƙasashe 15 da WHO ta tabbatar da cewa sun kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a. Waɗannan ƙasashen su ne Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuriyar Musulunci ta Iran, Jamhuriyar Dimokuradiyyar Jama'ar Lao, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo da Vanuatu. Kawar da cutar Trachoma ta amfani da dabarun kariya na hukumar lafiya ta duniya wato WHO SAFE strategy. Dukkan ƙasashen na Benin da Mali sun aiwatar da dabarun SAFE da WHO ta ba da shawarar amfani da su don kawar da cutar ta trachoma tare da tallafin WHO da ƙungiyoyin haɗin guiwa. Dabarar SAFE ta ƙunshi tiyata don magance matsalolin trachoma a matakin ƙarshe; maganin rigakafi don warkar da infekshan; tsabtar fuska; da inganta muhalli, musamman inganta samun ruwa da tsaftar muhalli, don rage yaɗuwa.

**Translation:**

WHO ta mika ta'aziyya da kuma amarya ga Benin da Mali bisa kawar da cutar trachoma a matsayin matsala ta lafiya a jama'a. Kasashen da aka amince da su a baya don kawar da cutar trachoma sun haɗa da Ghana (Yuni 2018), Gambia (Afril 2021), Togo (Mayu 2022) da Malawi (Satumba 2022).

“WHO ta mika ta’aziyya ga hukumomin lafiya na Benin da Mali da kuma kungiyoyin abokantaka da suka hada gwiwa a duniya da a cikin gida bisa wannan burin,” in ji Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Babban Daraktan WHO. “Bayan nasarar Benin da Mali, cutar trachoma har yanzu na ci gaba a kasashen 23 a yankin Afirka na WHO, wanda ya nuna cewa muna ci gaba da hanyar kawar da cutar trachoma da aka kafa a cikin kundin layi don cutan tropikal mara kulawa 2021–2030.”

A duniya, Benin da Mali sun shiga kasashen 15 da WHO ta amince da su bisa kawar da cutar trachoma a matsayin matsala ta lafiya a jama'a. Kasashen sun haɗa da Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuriyar Musulunci ta Iran, Jamhuriyar Kan China, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudiyya, Togo da Vanuatu.

**Kawar da cutar trachoma ta amfani da dabarar SAFE ta WHO**

Benin da Mali sun aiwatar da dabarar SAFE da WHO ta bayar da shawarar don kawar da cutar trachoma tare da taimakon WHO da abokan aikanta. Dabarar SAFE ta ƙunshi aikin tiyattewa don magance cutar trachoma a mataki na karshe; antibiotics don tsaftace cuta; tsaftace fuskoki; da inganta yanayin muhalli, musamman inganta samun ruwa da tsafta, don rage yaduwar cuta.

*   **"Magance cutar trachoma a mataki na karshe"**: This translates to "treating late trachoma complications."

*   **SAFE Strategy:** I kept "SAFE" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

translations.source: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD. “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.reference: Ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative ta ƙasa da ƙasa, kamfanin Pfizer ya ba da gudummuwar maganin azithromycin don aiwatar da tsarin na SAFE. Ƙasar Benin ta haɗa dabarun kawar da cutar ta trachoma tare da waɗanda aka aiwatar kan wasu cututtuka na wurare masu zafi da ba a kula da su (NTDs), a ƙarƙashin inuwar Hukumar Kula da Cututtuka masu Yaɗuwa ta Ƙasa. Trachoma ita ce cutar wurare masu zafi da ba kula da su (NTD) ta uku da aka kawar da ita a Benin, bayancutar dracunculiasis (a shekarar 2009) da cutar gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (a shekarar 2021). Ƙasar Mali ta gudanar da binciken kwakwaf game da tasiru da sa ido tare da ƙaddamar da matakan shawo kan matsalar, duk da ƙalubalen tsaro a yankunan arewacin ƙasar da kuma tashe-tashen hankula a tsakanin al'umma a 'yan shekarun nan. Cutar ta Trachoma ita ce ta cutar wurare masu zafi da ba kula da su (NTD) ta farko da aka kawar a ƙasar Mali, don haka yanzu ta shiga cikin rukunin ƙasashe 47 na duniya waɗanda suka kawar da aƙalla cutar wurare masu zafi da ba kula da su (NTD) guda ɗaya. "Waɗannan nasarori ne masu ban sha'awa a fannin kiwon lafiyar jama'a, in ji Dr Ibrahima Soce Fall, Daraktan shirin na WHO Global NTD. "Benin da Mali sun nuna yadda jajircewa a gwamnatance, haɗin kan ɓangarori daban-daban, sa ido da kuma haɗa kan al'umma ke aiki tare don cimma nasarar kawar da cututtuka." An samu gagarumin ci gaba a yaƙi da cutar ta trachoma a cikin 'yan shekarun da suka gabata. Adadin mutanen da ke buƙatar maganin cutar ta trachoma a yankin da WHO ke kula da shi a Afirka ya ragu da miliyan 84, daga miliyan 189 a shekarar 2014 zuwa miliyan 105 a watan Yunin 2022. Tsokaci ga Edita: Trachoma har yanzu matsalar lafiyar jama'a a cikin ƙasashe 41 (a watan Yuni 2022) inda kimanin mutane miliyan 125 da ke zaune a yankunan ke buƙatar yin rigakafin cutar.

translations.source: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person. Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.reference: Ana samun cutar ta Trachoma ne musamman a yankunan karkara mafi talauci a Afirka, Tsakiya da Kudancin Amurka, Asiya, Yammacin Pacific da Gabas ta Tsakiya. Yankin Afirka da WHO ke lura da shi yana fama da cutar ta trachoma, inda mutane miliyan 105 ke zaune a yankunan da ke fuskantar haɗarin, wanda ke wakiltar kashi 84% adadin masu cutar ta trachoma a duniya. Trachoma ita ce cutar da ke kan gaba wajen haifar da makanta a duniya. Cutar na samuwa ne sakamakon kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar Chlamydia trachomatis. Cutar tana yaɗuwa tsakanin mutane ne ta hanyar ta gurɓatattun hannaye, kayan sawa da na amfani da ƙudaje waɗanda suka yi mu'amala ruwan da ke fita daga idanu ko hancin mai cutar. Ababen hatsari da ke tattare da muhalli da ke haddasa yaɗuwar cutar ta trachoma sun haɗa da rashin tsafta, cunkoso a gidaje, rashin isasshen ruwa ko wuraren amfani masu tsafta. A shekara 1996, WHO ta ƙaddamar da Gidauniyar Haɗaka don kawar da Trachoma ta Duniya nan da shekara ta 2020 (GET2020). Tare da sauran abokan tarayya a cikin Gidauniyar ta haɗaka, WHO ba da goyon baya wajen aiwatar da dabarun SAFE da kuma ƙarfafa yunƙurin ƙasa ta hanyar tantance cututtuka, lura da sa ido, tantance ayyuka da tattara kayan aiki da ke taimakawa wajen kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a. Tsarin gudanarwar, wanda Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da shi a shekarar 2020 ta hanyar shawara mai lamba 73 (33) da ka yanke, ta sanya 2030 a matsayin sabon lokacin da ake nufin kawar da cutar a faɗin duniya.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a public health context. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Cutarsa mai ciwon ido (Trachoma) yawanci ana samu a mafi wahalar tattalin arziƙi da kuma ƙananan kauyuka a nahiyoyin Afirka, Amurka ta Tsakiya da Kuduga, Asiya, Pacific ta Yamma da Gabas, da kuma Gabas ta Tsakiya.  Kwamitin Kwalliyar Lafiya na Duniya (WHO) na Afirka ya fi jiniɓance a kan cutar trachoma, da mutane miliyan 105 da ke rayuwa a yankuna masu haɗɗaci, wanda ya ƙunshi kashi 84% na cutar trachoma a duniya. Cutar trachoma ita ce babban abin da ke haifar da lalɓar ido ta cuta a duniya. Cutar ta samo asali ne ta hanyar kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar Chlamydia trachomatis. Kamuwa ta yaduwa daga mutum zuwa mutum ta hanyar yatsan da suka tashi da ƙarya, abubuwan da ake amfani da su (fomites), da ƙwama waɗanda suka taba fitar da ido ko kunƙushen mutum mai ciwo. Ƙungiyoyin haɗin gwiwa na muhimman yanayi don yaduwar trachoma sun haɗa da ƙaƙƙarfan tsabta, gine-gine da yawa a gida, rashin samun isassun ruwa ko amfani da kayan aikin tsafta. A shekarar 1996, WHO ta ƙirƙiri Kungiyar WHO don kawar da cutar trachoma a duniya zuwa shekarar 2020 (GET2020). Tare da sauran abokan tarayya a kungiyar, WHO tana tallafawa aiwatar da ƙa'ida ta SAFE a ƙasashe da ƙarfafa ƙarfin kasa ta hanyar aiyuka, bincike, saka ido, ƙimaɗi da ƙididdigar albarkatunci don kawo ƙarshen cutar trachoma a matsayin matsala ta lafiya ta jama'a.  Dabarar, da Majalisar Kwalliyar Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da ita a shekarar 2020 ta hanyar ƙa'idar 73(33), ta ƙayyade shekarar 2030 a matsayin sabon kwanan wani don kawar da cutar trachoma a duniya.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Cutarsa mai ciwon ido (Trachoma)"**: I used "Cutarsa mai ciwon ido" which translates to "The disease of the eye" and included "Trachoma" in parentheses for clarity, especially for those familiar with the English term.

*   **Word Choice:** I tried to use relatively common and accessible Hausa vocabulary, avoiding overly technical terms where possible, while still maintaining accuracy.

translations.source: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.reference: Jarirai miliyan 152 ne aka haifa kafin lokacin haihuwarsu ya yi. 1. A cikin kowane jarirai 10 da ake haifa, 1 bakwaini ne - kuma kowane sakan 40, 1 na waɗannan jariran yana mutuwa. Yawan jariran da ake haifa kafin lokacin haihuwarsu ya yi bai sauya ba a cikin shekaru goma da suka wuce a duk sassa na duniya. Tasirin rikice-rikice da na sauyin yanayi da ma na cutar Covid-19 duk ƙarin ƙalubale ne ga mata da jarirai a ko'ina. Akwai buƙatar ɗaukar matakan gaggawa domin dakatar da haihuwar jarirai kafin lokaci ya yi tare da samar da cikakkiyar kulawa ga jariran tare da iyalan da hakan ya auku gare su. Bisa ƙiyasi, jarirai miliyan 13.4 aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata a shekarar 2020 kuma kusan su miliyan 1 daga cikinsu suka rasu a saboda hakan, a cewar wani rahoton da ma'aikatun Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya suka fitar a kwanan nan. Wannan adadi daidai yake da 1 cikin jararai 10 da aka haifa da wuri (kafin mako 37 da ɗaukar ciki) a duniya baki ɗaya. Daftarin Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth wanda Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta samar da haɗin guiwar Hukumar Kula da Ƙananan Yara ta Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya wato UNICEF tare da PMNCH wadda ita ce haɗaka mafi girma tsakanin mata da yara da kuma matasa a duk faɗin duniya ya abin da ke buƙatar ɗaukin gaggawa da ba san da shi ba na haihuwar jarirai kafin lokacin da ya kamata, wanda ba a fahimci yawansa da tsananinsa ba, wanda kuma yana dakushe cigaba da ma rayuwar jarirai. Rahoton ya ƙunshi bayanai da aka samo daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da ta UNICEF wanda makarantar kula da tsafta da cutuka a yankuna masu zafi ta Landan ta tsara a kan yawaitar jariran da ake haihuwa kafin lokacin da ya kamata. A gaba ɗaya, ta gano cewa yawan jariran da ake haifa kafin lokaci bai sauya ba a kowane yanki na duniya a cikin shekaru goma da suka wuce, inda aka samu jarirai miliyan 152 da aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata tsakanin shekarar 2010 zuwa ta 2020.

**Translation (Hausa):**

A shekarar goma da suka gabata, miliyan 152 na jarirai sun haihu a gaba da lokaci. Daga kowace jarirai 10 da aka haihu, daya ya haihu a gaba da lokaci – kuma kowane dakika 40, daya daga wadannan jarirai ya mutu.  Kwamshin haihuwa na gaba da lokaci bai canza a cikin shekarar goma da suka gabata a kowace yankin duniya ba.  Tasirin rikici, canjin yanayi, da COVID-19 na kara haɗin gaskiya ga mata da jarirai a ko'ina cikin duniya.  Ya zama dole a dauki mataki nan da nan don inganta hana haihuwa a gaba da lokaci, da kuma inganta kula da jarirai da suka shafi da iyalansu.

Kima miliyan 13.4 na jarirai sun haihu a gaba da lokaci a shekarar 2020, kuma kusan miliyan 1 sun mutu sakamakon cutar da ke faruwa a gaba da lokaci, a cewar rahoton sabo da aka fitar a yau ne ta hukumomin Majalisar Dinkin Duniya da abokantaka.  Wannan ya yi daidawa da kusan daya a cikin kowace jarirai 10 da aka haihu a gaba da lokaci (kadanin mako 37 na juna biyu) a duniya.

"Haifu da sauri: shekarar aiki kan haihuwa a gaba da lokaci," wanda aka shirya ta Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da Kungiyar Kyautato Mazauna Duniya (UNICEF) tare da PMNCH -- mafi girman kungiyar a duniya don mata, yara, da matasa, na kiran hankali kan "gajeriyar gaggawa" na haihuwa a gaba da lokaci, wacce ta kasance a baya a gane girman da tsanowinta, wacce ke hana ci gaba da inganta lafiya da rayuwar yara.  Rahoton ya haɗa kima na sabuntawa daga WHO da UNICEF, da aka shirya tare da Makarantar Kwararin Ilimi ta Landan da Kwararin Jima'i, akan yawan haihuwa a gaba da lokaci.  Gaba daya, rahoton ya gano cewa ƙwamshin haihuwa a gaba da lokaci bai canza a kowace yankin duniya ba a cikin shekarar goma da suka gabata, tare da miliyan 152 na jarirai marasa taimako da suka haihu a gaba da lokaci daga 2010 zuwa 2020.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Preterm"**: I used "a gaba da lokaci" which translates to "before time" or "premature." This is a common and understandable way to express this concept in Hausa.
*   **Statistics**: I tried to maintain the numerical accuracy and clarity of the statistics.
*   **Organizations**: I kept the acronyms (WHO, UNICEF, PMNCH) as they are often used internationally, but provided the full names in parentheses the first time they appear.
*   **"Silent Emergency"**: I translated this as "gajeriyar gaggawa" which means "short emergency" or "brief crisis."  It captures the sense of a hidden and ongoing problem.
*   **"Vulnerable Babies"**: I used "jarirai marasa taimako" which means "helpless babies" or "babies in need of help."
*   **Word Choice**: I aimed for a balance between formal and accessible language, suitable for a general audience.
*   **Flow and Readability**: I've structured the sentences to flow naturally in Hausa.

**Important Considerations:**

translations.source: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive. The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.reference: Haihuwar jarirai kafin lokacin da ya kamata shi ne yanzu ya fi haddasa mace-macen yara, inda yake haddasa mutuwar yaro guda duk cikin su 5 da suke rasu kafin su shekara 5. Jariran da aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata sukan fuskanci ƙalubale na tsawon rayuwa, inda sukan iya samun wata naƙasa ko kuma rashin girma yadda ya kamata. Bambancin rayuwa dangane da yanki ko matsayin tattalin arziƙi ko kuma launin fata. La'akari da wani shahararren rahoto game da lamarin a shekara ta 2012, wannan daftarin na Born too soon ya fayyace yadda ake samuwar jarirai bakwani (waɗanda aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata) dalla-dalla tare da tasirin da hakan yake da shi a kan mata da iyalai da al'umma da kuma tattalin arziƙi. A yawancin lokuta, inda aka haifi jarirai shi yake tasiri wajen rayuwarsu ko rashinta. Rahoton ya yi nuni da cewa, jariri 1 ne kawai a cikin 10 ke rayuwa idan an haife shi a lokacin da ko kusa lokacin haihuwarsa bai yi ba (ƙasa da mako 28 da ɗaukar ciki) a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi, saɓanin a ƙasashe masu arziƙi inda adadin ya kai jararai 9 a cikin 10. Cike giɓin rashin daidaiton da ake samu ta fuskar launin fata da ƙabila da tattalin arziƙi da ma samun hanyoyin kiwon lafiya nagartattu shi zai taimaka a rage samun jarirai bakwaini da rage mace-macensu da kuma samun naƙasarsu hatta a ƙasashe masu yalwar arziƙi. Kudancin nahiyar Asiya da yankin Afirka kudu da Sahara su suka fi yawan jarirai bakwaini sannan kuma a nan ne aka fi samun mutuwarsu. Waɗannan yankuna guda biyu idan aka tara su sun kwashe kashi 65 a cikin ɗari na yawan jarirai bakwaini na duk faɗin duniya. Har'ilayau, rahoton ya bayyana cewa tasirin rikice-rikice da na sauyin yanayi da gurɓacewar muhalli da na cutar Covid-19 dakuma tsadar rayuwar barazan ce ga mata da jarirai a ko'ina.

Haɓakar haihuwa (preterm birth) yanzu haka ita ce babban abin da ke haifar da mutuwar yara, ta fi kowane abu fiye da ɗaya a cikin kowace goma (1 a cikin 5) na mutuwar yara da ke faruwa kafin ranar haihuwa ta su ta hanyar shekara biyar.  Masu haɓaka haihuwa na iya fuskantar matsala da lafiya a rayuwarsu, tare da babban yuwuwar lalacewa da jinkunai a ci gaba da su.

**Rarrabuwar rayuwarsu bisa yanki, kudin shiga, launin kai**

Daga rahoton tarihi kan wannan batu a shekarar 2012, wannan sabon rahoton "Born too soon" na shekaru goma yana ba da babban bayani kan yawan haɓaka haihuwa da kuma tasirinsa na sosai akan mata, iyalai, al'umma da kuma tattalin arzaki. Babban bambanci da ke faruwa bisa launi, asali, kudin shiga da kuma samun kulawar lafiya mai kyau ya ƙara yuwuwar haɓaka haihuwa, mutuwar da lalacewa, har ma a kasashe da ke da kudin shiga mai yawa. translations.source: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.reference: Misali, an ƙiyasta cewa gurɓatacciyar iska kawai tana haddasa haihuwar jarirai bakaini miliyan 6 a duk shekara. A wani sabon nazari na rahoton an fahimci cewa, kusan bakaini 1 a duk su 10 da aka haifa za a iske yana a cikin ƙasashen da yawan rikice-rikice ya fi addabar su ne. Matsalolin laulayin ciki da suke samuwa sakamakon ɗaukar ciki da wuri ga budurwa ko hawan jinin da ya samu sanadiyar juna-biyu suna da dangantaka sosai da haihuwar bakwaini. Wannan ya sa akwai buƙatar a inganta kula da lafiya ta fuskar zamantakewar aure da samun haihuwa, ciki har da tazarar haihuwa da samar da cikakkiyar kulawa ga mata masu ciki da kuma lokacin haihuwarsu. Matakan da za a ɗauka:ƙasashe su ƙara zuba jari sannan iyaye su kuma su fito domin yin gwagwarmaya a kan lamarin. A shekaru goma da suka gabata, an kuma samu cigaba ta fuskar gwagwarmayar al'umma kan rage haihuwar bakwaini da yin ɓarin ciki, inda iyaye da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da manyan makarantu da ƙungiyoyi masu fafutuka da sauransu suka shiga tsundum aka dama da su. A duk faɗin duniya, ƙungiyoyin iyalai na waɗanda suka haifi bakwaini a baya su ne a kan gaba wajen yin kira da a inganta kula tare da sanya tsare-tsare sannan kuma da taimaka wa wasu iyalan da hakan ya auku gare su. Har zuwa lokacin da aka gudanar d Taron Ƙara wa Juna Ilimi a Kan Kula da Jarirai da aka yi a Cape Town ta Afirka ta Kudu tsakanin 8-11 ga watan Mayu, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da ta UNICEF da ta UNFPA da ku ta PMNCH duk sun yi ta kiraye-kiraye kan inganta lafiyar mata da ta jarirai da kuma lalubo hanyoyin daƙile haihuwar bakwaini kamar haka: Ƙara zuba jari: Samar da kuɗaɗe daga ciki da wajen ƙasa domin inganta lafiyar mata masu ciki da jarirai ta hanyar tabbatar da an bayar da kulawar da ta dace a wurare mabuƙata kuma a lokacin da ya dace. Gaggauta aiwatarwa: Cimma burin ƙasa na cigaba ta hanyar aiwatar da tsare-tsaren da aka yi na inganta lafiyar mata masu ciki da jarirai.

**Hausa Translation:**

Misali, ana kiyayyata cewa cutar isasshen ruwan sama na haifar da bayi miliyan 6 da suka haihu kafin lokacinsu kowace shekara. Kusan bayi ɗaya a cikin goma na bayi da suka haihu kafin lokaci suna faruwa a kasashe goma mafi kasala wacce ke fuskantar rikici na bil'adama, kamar yadda sabon bincike ya nuna a rahoton. Matsalolin lafiya ga mata, irin su junaɓu da haihuwa a shekarun ƙanƙanta da pre-eclampsia, suna da dangantaka mai ƙarfi da haihuwa kafin lokaci. Wannan ya jaddada buƙatar tabbatar da samun isassun ayyukan lafiya da kuma lafiya na jima'i da haihuwa, gami da tsara iyalewa mai tasiri, tare da kula da inganci a lokacin junaɓu da kusa da lokacin haihuwa.

**Agenda don aiki: Karuwar saka hannun jari na kasa da kuma kokawar iyaye**

Karni da ya wuce, mun ga karuwar kokawar al'umma kan hana haihuwa kafin lokaci da mutuwar yara saboda rashin sahan, ta hanyar kungiyoyin iyaye, ma'aikata, malamai, kungiyoyin farar haka, da sauran mutane. A duniya dukkanin, kungiyoyin da ke taimakon iyalai da suka shafi haihuwa kafin lokaci sun kasance a gaba wajen neman samun isassun kula da canjin manufa kuma suna tallafawa sauran iyalai.

A gaban Taron Kasa da Kasa kan Lafiyar Mahaifa da Sabo, a Cape Town, Afirka kudu daga 8-11 Mayu, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA da PMNCH suna kiran aiwatar da ayyukan da ke gaba don inganta kula da mata da sabo kuma don rage haɗarin da ya taso sakamakon haihuwa kafin lokaci:

*   **Ƙara aiwatar da ayyuka:** Cika burin kasashe don ci gaba ta hanyar aiwatar da manufofin kasa da suka kafa don kula da mahaifa da sabo.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Preterm Births":** I used "Bayi da suka haihu kafin lokacinsu" which translates to "babies born before their time." This is a common and understandable way to express this in Hausa.
*   **"Humanitarian Crises":** Translated as "rikici na bil'adama" (humanitarian crisis).
*   **"Sexual and Reproductive Health Services":** Translated as "ayyukan lafiya da kuma lafiya na jima'i da haihuwa" (health services and sexual and reproductive health).
*   **"Parent-led Activism":** Translated as "kokawar iyaye" (parent's activism/effort).
*   **"Mobilizing Resources":** Translated as "Mobilizing kusan kudin da ke kasawa a duniya da kuma a gida" (mobilizing almost the money that is spending in the world and in the country).

translations.source: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon. While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.reference: Ƙarfafa dukkan ɓangarori: Tallafa wa ilimi a dukkan matakan rayuwa, samar da tallafi ta fuskar tattalin arziƙi a dukkan fannoni, ƙarfafa wa jama'a fuskantar sauyin yanayi sannan kuma da ƙarfafa tare-tsaren taimakon gaggawa. Samar da ƙirƙire-ƙirƙiren cikin gida: Zuba jari a ƙirƙire-ƙirƙire da bincike na cikin gida domin taimaka wa cigaba ta fuskar inganta tare da daidaita samar da kulawa gare su. Tsakure: Farfesa Joy Lawn, Makarantar Kula da Tsafta da Samar da Magunguna ga Ƙasashe masu Yanayin Zafi ta Landan. Jagorantar Daftarin Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023)da na Born too soon (2012). "Wannan sabon rahoton ya nuna cewa rashin ɗaukar matakan da suka dace ya haddasa haiuwar jarirai bakwaini har miliyan 152. Duk da yake wasu yankuna sun fi fuskantar matsalar, amma haihuwar yara bakwaini ƙalubale ne ga kiwon lafiya a kowace ƙasa. Ƙarin zuba jari a kan kula da jarirai zai taimaka wajen tsare iyalai da dama daga damuwa. Akwai kuma buƙatar yin aiki tuƙuru domin hana haihuwar jarirai bakwaini wanda hakan kuma zai taimaka wajen rage yin ɓari da mutuwar mata masu ciki. Waɗannan tagwayen hanyoyi na hana haihuwar jarirai bakwaini idan suka haɗu, za su taimaka wajen samar da lafiyayyun mutane da al'ummomi waɗanda za su kawo wa ƙasa cigaba ta fuskar tattalin arziƙi da zamantakewa. Ya'ya da jikokinmu sun dogara da abin da muka yi a yau --- jarin da za mu saka ko da ba mai yawa ba ne zai iya zama mai yawa ga kowace ƙasa.

translations.source: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.” Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.reference: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Ɗaya daga cikin marubuta na Born Too Soon sannan kuma jagora na Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) na ƙasa. A yanzu kenan babu dalili na yin shiru. Yanzu muna da wuƙa da nama da ilimi na sauya makomar jariranmu da wannan abin zai iya shafa. Ina mai alfahari da ganin a yanzu gwamnatin ƙasata ta mayar da hankali a kan aiwatarwa hatta a yankunan karkara. Shekaru goma masu zuwa suna iya zama, kai dole ne ma su zama daban dangane da masu fuskantar haihuwar bakwaini. Dr Anshu Banerjee, Darektan kula da lafiyar mata masu ciki da jarirai da yara da matasa da harkar da ta shafi tsufa na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO). Tabbatar da an samar da ingantacciyar kulawa ga waɗannan mitsitsan jarirai da iyalansu ya zama wajibi domin inganta kula da yara da rage mace-macensu. Akwai buƙatar samun cigaba ta fuskar kula da jarirai bakwaini --- wannan na nufin cewa dole kowace mace mai ciki ta iya samun damar halartar asibiti kafin da kuma yayin da take ɗauke da juna biyu domin gano tare da fuskantar ƙalubale. Steven Lauwerier, Darektan Kiwon Lafiya (a.i.), UNICEF Babban rashi da damuwa ne ke biyo bayan dukkan mutuwar jariri bakwaini.

translations.source: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five. Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.reference: Duk da cigaba da aka samu a duniya ta fuskoki da dama, babu wata cigaba ta a-zo-a-gani da aka samu dangane da yawan jarirai bakwaini da kuma rage mace-macensu. Yawan yana razanarawa. Lokaci ya yi na inganta lafiyar mata masu ciki da kuma jarirai bakwaini domin tabbatar da samun ingantacciyar lafiya da kyakkyawar rayuwa gare su. Helga Fogstad, Babban Darekta na PMNCH Daftarin Born too soon ya nuna wajibcin ƙara zuba jari da sa-ido yadda ya kamata a kan haihuwar jarirai bakwaini wanda shi ne babban dalilin mutuwar yara 'yan ƙasa da shekara biyar. Cigaban da ake samu dangane da kiwon lafiyar mata masu ciki da na jarirai da ma samuwar ɓarin ciki na mai haƙin rijiya ne, ga shi kuma ya ƙara samun koma-baya sakamakon cutar Covid-19 da sauyin yanayi da ƙaruwar rikice-rikice da kuma tsadar rayuwa. Idan muka haɗa kai, gwamnatocinmu da masu hannu da shuni da ma'aikatu masu zaman kansu da ƙungiyoyi masu fafutuka da iyaye da kuma ma'aikatan lafiya, za mu iya fito da wannan abin da ke buƙatar gaggawa har ya zama cikin muhimman abin da ɓangaren kiwon lafiya na ƙasa ya sa a gaba ta yadda za a gina al'umma ta hanyar tallafa wa iyalai da ƙungiyoyi da ma ɓangarori na tattalin arziƙi a ko'ina. "Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: iyaye ga Jalen Foster, kuma waɗanda suka kafa Jalen's Gift Foundation An kafa gidauniyarmu ne bayan an da muka rasa ɗanmu mai suna Jalen Foster wanda bakwaini ne da aka haifa tun cikinsa yana mako 27 sakamakon harbuwar da ya yi da ƙwayar cuta mai suna streptococous nau'I na B. Rashin ɗan namu ya kasance babban tashin hankali a gare mu kuma babu wanda a tallafa mana daga cikin al'umma, don haka muka ga zai dace a faranta wa iyalan da suka rasa ɗa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

"Kodayake duniya ta cimaka mun isowar ci gaban da dama a shekarar da ta gabata, ba mu ci gaba da raguwa kan adadin jarirai kanana da ke haihuwa ba bisa ga alkibidi, ko kuma hana barazanar mutuwar su. Matsalacin da ya taso ya cutar da mutane sosai. Ya zuwa lokaci da za mu inganta samun damamn kula ga mata da ke jima'i da ciki da jarirai da suka haihu ba bisa ga alkibidi, kuma tabbatar da cewa kowannen yaro ya sami farawa mai kyau kuma ya kasance lafiya a rayuwa." Helga Fogstad, Babban Darakta, PMNCH

"Haifuwa ba bisa ga alkibidi ya nuna dalilin da ya sa dole mu ƙara saka kuɗi da kuma yiwa kuskuren haihuwa ba bisa ga alkibidi - babban abin da ke haifar da mutuwar yara 'yan kasa da shekara biyar. Ci gaba a kan lafiya ta mahaifa da sabo yana ragowa, kuma hana mutuwar yara a ciki yana janyo ƙara ta wahala ta amsar haɗuwana da cutar COVID-19, canjin yanayi, yakin kananan kasashe da kuma haɓaka farashin rayuwa.

"Ta hanyar aiki tare a matsayin abokan tarayya - gwamnati, masu ba da kuɗi, ɓangaren dangi, jami'an kungiyoyin farar haka, iyaye, da ƙwararrun lafiya - za mu yi kuka game da wannan "gajeriyar matsala", kuma mu kawo ƙoƙarin hana haihuwa ba bisa ga alkibidi da kula da su a gabaɗaya a cikin ƙoƙarin lafiya da ci gaban kasa, da ƙirƙirar babban ɗan adam ta hanyar tallafawa iyalai, al'umma da kuma tattalin arzaki a ko'ina." Gabriela da Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, Amurka: iyayen Jalen Foster, da wadattauran Jalen's Gift Foundation

"Kwamatinmu ta kulla bayan rasuwar danmu, Jalen Foster, wanda ya mutu a ciki a shekarar 27 saboda cutar da ta fara ne a cikin ciki wacce ake kira da group B streptococcus. Rasa danmu ya cutar da mu sosai, kuma babu wata hanyar tallafi a al'umma, kuma shi ne lokacin da muka yanke shawarar samar da wuri mai dacewa da kuma alkawari ga iyalai waɗanda suka rasa jariri."

---

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Born too soon"**: I translated this as "Haifuwa ba bisa ga alkibidi" which means "Birth before the due date/preterm birth." It's the most accurate and commonly used term in Hausa.

*   **"Partnership"**: Translated as "abokan tarayya" (partners/allies).
*   **"COVID-19"**: Kept as "cutar COVID-19" as it's commonly used in Hausa.
*   **"Group B streptococcus"**: Kept as "group B streptococcus" as it's a technical term and often used as is.
*   **Flow and Naturalness:** I've tried to ensure the Hausa reads naturally and avoids overly literal translations that can sound awkward. I've adjusted sentence structures where necessary to fit Hausa grammar.

translations.source: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.reference: Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya sun kai wani jirgin sama mai dauke da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya zuwa Sudan Haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) sun kai wani jirgin sama mai ɗauke da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya zuwa ƙasar Sudan. wani jirgi ɗauke da kayayyakin jinya da tiyatar gaggawa da muhimman magunguna sun isa filin jirgin saman port Sudan da safiyar yau. kayan waɗanda darajar su ta kai dalar Amurka 444 000, shine na farko da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta iya kaiwa ta jirgin sama zuwa Sudan tun bayan ɓarkewar rikicin. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta rarraba kayayyakin kowon lafiya kafin ƙaruwar rikicin. Waɗancan sun ƙare ne ƴan kwanaki kaɗan bayan an bada adadin waɗanda suka jikkata. Aikewa da jirgin na zuwa ne a wani mataki na cigaba da bada agajin da haɗaɗaɗiyya daular larabawa(UAE) ke yi na bada tallafawa wa al’ummar Sudan da ke nuna ƙarfafa dangatakar ta da ƙasar Sudan, musamman ma ta la’akari da halin da ƙasar ke ciki a halin yanzu. Har ila yau faɗaɗa ayyukan jinƙai na haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa tare da jadadda alaƙar ta da sauran ƙasashe dangane da ƴan uwantaka da jajircewarta na taimakon wasu a lokutan rikici da halin gaggawa. Ƙaramar ministar haɗin gwiwar ƙasa da ƙasa Dakta Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy tace haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa (UAE) na cigaba da yin aiki kafaɗa da kafaɗa da hukumar lafiya ta duniya domin tabbatar da nasarar tura muhimman ayyuka da dabarun ayyukan agajin gaggawa da magunguna da abinci don taimakawa wajen magance halin da ake ciki a yanzu na rikicin Sudan. A daidai lokacin da ake kira ga dukkan rikice rikice da juna da su gaggauta tsagaita wuta tare da kawar da matsalar da ke janyo wa al'ummar Sudan wahalhalun da ba za a iya mantawa da su ba, Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa ta tsaya tsayin daka wajen bayar da taimako da taimako ga kasashe a lokutan bukata.

translations.source: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.reference: Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa ta mayar da hankali ne musamman wajen samar wa kungiyoyin da suka fi fama da matsalar halin da ake ciki a Sudan, musamman ma marasa lafiya, da yara, da tsoffi, da mata wadanda suka fi fuskantar hadarin daga rikice-rikicen da ke ci gaba da faruwa. mafi matsananciyar gibi a cikin samar da magunguna da abinci. Ƙididdigar jin ƙai na Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa tana nufin cewa ta tabbatar da cewa ta ci gaba da isar da saƙo ga duniya ƙaƙƙarfan sadaukarwarta ga ƙarfafa zaman lafiya, tsaro da kwanciyar hankali, ba kawai a wani yanki ba, har ma a duk faɗin duniya, tare da abokan hulɗarta da al'ummomin duniya. kasar na ci gaba da taimakawa al'ummar Sudan a lokutan rikici. Kayayyakin kiwon lafiyar sun haɗa da isassun raunuka, kayan aikin tiyata na gaggawa, da magunguna masu mahimmanci don kai ga mutane 165,000 da ke cikin matsananciyar buƙatar agajin jin kai. Cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya a faɗin ƙasar sun ba da rahoton cewa, kayayyakin kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum sun ƙare kuma basa samun damar shiga shaguna saida magunguna a ƙasar. Daga cikin tallafin hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) ta tura ƙwararrun likitocin gaggawa guda biyu da ke tafiya tare da tabbatar da cewa an rarraba waɗannan kayayyaki nan take zuwa manyan cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya goma sha uku don tallafawa wa ma’aikatan kiwon lafiya da kuma bada kulawa ga masu buƙatar agaji. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) tana da tan talatin na kayan agaji na zazzaɓin cizon sauro da cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa kamar ciwon suga hawan jini da kuma yanayin da ka iya haifar da asarar rai matuƙar ba’a ɗauki mataki ba. An gama shirya waɗannan haɗe da wajen ledar jini 23,000 a kamfanin jigila na duniya na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya a International Humanitarian City, sannan Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya tana ta neman hanyoyin da za ta kai waɗannan kaya zuwa Sudan da gaggawa, tare da haɗin guiwar Ma'aikatar Lafiya ta Sudan. Babban daraktan shirin agajin gaggawa ta hukumar lafiya ta duniya Dakta Micheal Ryan yace shirin agajin gaggawa na kamafani na jigila na duniya na hukumar lafiya ta duniya da haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa ta zama cibiyar samar da taimakon gaggawa a faɗin duniya. Taimakawa wa ƙasashe sama da ɗari da arba’in na a yankuna shida na hukumar lafiya ta duniya kamfanin na samar da hanyar rayuwa ga mambobin jihohin da ke fuskantar matsalolin kiwon lafiya da suka taso sanadiyyar ɓarkewar cututtuka bala’o’i kamar ambaliyar ruwa girgizar ƙasa da kuma tasirin rikicin akan tsarin kiwon lafiya. Tallafin da haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa ke bayarwa ya baiwa hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) kasancewa a sahun gaba a matakin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya da kuma isar da ƙwararrun likitoci ga masu buƙata a ko’ina a kuma duk lokacin da ake buƙata.

translations.source: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies. It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.reference: A ƙoƙarinta na gana ƙaƙƙarfan tsarin agajin gaggawa a faɗin duniya kulawa da kuma jajircewa haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa (UAE) da kuma hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) da abokan haɗin gwiwar suna aiki don isar da kayayyakin don isa ga mutane masu rauni a duniya a lokacin da suke kan buƙata A nasa ɓangaren Daktan hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) na yankin gabashin bahar rum Dakta Ahmed Al-Mandhari yace hukumar lafiya na cigaba da haɗa kai da hukumomin kiwon lafiya na Sudan domin isar da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya daga dukkan hanyoyin da ake da su. Sakamakon rikicin asibitoci kantinan magani da cibiyoyin kula da lafiya a matakin farko sun tanadi muhimman maguguna don ceton rai. Zuwan ƙarin tan talatin na kayyakin agaji da tiyatar gaggawa da kuma muhimman magunguna zasu ba hukumar lafiya ta duniya damar tallafawa hukumomin lafiya goma sha uku da sake kafa ayyukan kula da lafiyar masu buƙatar hakan. Kamfanin mu na jigila na duniya a Dubai yana taka muhimmiyar rawa wurin tabbatar kayayyakin kiwon lafiya ba tare da katsewa ba kuma muna godiya da tallafin da haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa ta bamu don sauƙaƙe mana na farko. Irin wannan taimakon kamar wannan shi ne yake nuna ƙoƙari a aikacenmu na yanki na samar da lafiya ba kowa. Tallafin lafiya da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta bayar ta hannun haɗaɗɗiyar fdaular larabawa ya kai kusan kashi hamsin da biyar na jimilla kayayyakin jinya da aka baiwa Sudan daga ƙetare a shekarar 2022 wanda ke nuni da matsayin haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa a matsayin babbar cibiyar taimakon jinƙai ta ƙasa da ƙasa.

translations.source: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Haɗin kai a cikin gida shine mabuɗin don ci gaba a duniya game da kiwon lafiya na muradan ƙarni SDGs Hukumomin 13 da suka sanya hannu kan Tsarin Ayyukan Duniya don Rayuwar Lafiya da Jin Dadin Kowa (SDG3 GAP) ne ke fitar da wani sabon rahoton ci gaba tare a haɗin gwiwa a yau. Rahoton mai taken me ya faru? mene bai yi ba? menen na gaba yana nuna shekaru huɗu na ayyukan haɗin gwiwa da manyan hukumomin ƙungiyoyi suka yi tare da bayyana abinda yayi aiki da wanda bai yi aiki ba domin ƙarfafa haɗin gwiwa da tallafi domin cigaban ƙasa a ɓangaren harkokin lafiya da kuma cigaban muradan ƙarni. A wannan shekarar ce aka kai rabin cimma cigaban muradan ƙarni amma kuma duniya tana baya wurin cimma muradan ƙarnin. Tun lokacin da aka ƙaddamar da shi a cikin 2019, SDG3 GAP ya ba da damar ƙirƙirar sabbin tsarin haɗin gwiwa tsakanin hukumomin da suka sanya hannu a cikin mahimman fannoni kamar dorewar kuɗi da kula da lafiya na farko da sauransu. Mafi ƙaranci ƙasashe 67 ne suka shiga ɗaya ko fiye da ɗaya daga cikin shirye-shiryen ingantata fasali na SDG3 GAP. Mun samu cigaba mai muhimmanci amma akwai amma har yanzu akwai doguwar tafiya domin inganta yadda ƙungiyoyi daban daban ke aiki tare domin tallafa wa ƙasashe. Dole ne mu saurari abinda ƙasashe ke gaya mana kuma muyi aiki bisa jagorancin su inji Dakta Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus darakta janar na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kuma shugaban ƙungiyar shugabannin SDG3 GAP. Ina godiya ga abokan hulɗa bisa hadin kaida suka yi da kuma yadda suka nuna gaskiya cikin wannan rahoto. Rahoton ya bada shawarwari guda shida masu muhimmanci don tabbaatar da cewa ƙasashe sun ci gajiyar tallafin da ya dace da kuma tabbatar da alƙawuran da aka yi a SDG3 GAP.

**Hausa Translation:**

*Halin da ke faruwa a kanananan hukumanceshi na da matukar mahimmanci don ci gaban duniya bisa lafiya da kuma burin ci gaban kasashe (SDGs). Rahoton mai taken “Menene da ya yi aiki? Menene da bai yi aiki ba? Menene da za a yi sa’an nan?” ya nuna shekaru huɗu na aiki tare da manyan hukumomin kasa da kasa, da nuna abin da ya yi aiki da abin da bai yi aiki ba wajen ƙarfafa hadin gwiwa da tallafi don haɓaka ci gaban kasashe bisa lafiya.

Kwanan nan, mun kai tsakiyar hanyar zuwa burin SDGs, amma duniya na jinkiri a kai. Tun lokacin da aka kafa SDG3 GAP a shekarar 2019, ya haɓaka ƙirƙirar tsarin hadin gwiwa tsakanin hukumomin da suka kulla alƙawari a fannin da ke da mahimmanci, irin su kwarewar kuɗi da kuma kula da lafiya a matakin farko. Akalla kasashe 67 sun shiga wani ko wasu manyan jigon SDG3 GAP.

Muna buƙatar saurari abin da kasashe ke ce muna da aiwatar da jagorancin su,” in ji Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Babban Daraktan WHO da Shugaban Kungiyar Alkalai ta SDG3 GAP. “Na yi murnatiyan abokan tarayya bisa hadin gwiwarsu da kuma binciken kai na gaskiya da ke cikin wannan rahoton.”

Rahoton ya bayar da shawara guda shida don tabbatar da cewa kasashe suna amfanar da tallafin da ya fi sauƙi da kuma aiwatar da alƙawarin da aka yanke a ƙarƙashin SDG3 GAP.*

translations.source: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.reference: Sun haɗa da: Cigaba da inganta cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP na lafiya ta hanyar neman ra’ayoyin mambobin ƙasashe kan yadda muke haɗa kai a matakin ƙasa da kuma amsa shawarwarin da suka danganci; Kula da GAP a matsayin dandamali da haɗin gwiwa; Haɓaka haɗin gwiwa mai ƙarfi a matakin ƙasa kan kula da hukumar kiwon lafiya a matakin farko da kuma bincika sabbin batutuwa kamar tsarin kiwon lafiya mai jure yanayi Tare da yin amfani da sabbin hanyoyin a matakin ƙasa domin isar da tsari mai tasiri; Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Aiki tare da mambobin ƙasashe don ƙarfafa gwiwa ta hanyar jagoranci a siyasance da kuma jagoranci na shugabanci da kudi domin tallafawa da haɗin gwiwa. Mihimman binciken da aka samu na rahoton da aka samu na rahoton zai bada labari game da tattaunawar babban taron majalisar dinkin duniya na cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG da kuma taron duniya na akan kiwon lafiya a watan Satumbar 2023. Watanni kafin waɗannan muhimman abubuwan da suka faru sun bada dama ga haɗin gwiwar ƙasashe membobi da hukumomi da ƙungiyoyi daban daban don gano hanyoyin da za’a aiwatar da shawarwarin da shiryawa don yin ƙarfaffan haɗin gwiwa tare da haɓaka cigaba ga cigaban muradan ƙarni masu alaƙa da kiwon lafiya na rabin lokacin da SDG ta dauka. Bayanan kula ga Edita:

translations.source: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.reference: SDG GAP ya bayyana yadda hukumomi goma sha da suka sahannu zasu yi amfani da sabbin hanyoyin aiki, tare. Masu rattaba hannu ga ciga cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP sune Gavi, da kuma ƙungiyar allurar rigakafi Kayan Tallafi na Duniya don Mata, Yara da Matasa (GFF); Asusun duniya don yaƙi da cutar AIDS,tarin fuka da zazzaɓin cizon sauro(Asusun duniya) Ƙungiyar ƙwadago ta duniya (ILO); Shirin haɗin gwiwa na majalisar ɗinkin duniya kan HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); Shirin cigaban majalisar ɗinkin duniya (UNDP) Asusun majalisar ɗinkin duniya na yawan jama’a (UNFPA); Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Ƙungiyar majalisar ɗinkin duniya don daidaita jinsi da ƙarfafa mata (matan majalisar ɗinkin duniya)

translations.source: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.reference: Sabon sakamakon binciken ya nuna tsarin kiwon lafiya ya fara murmurewa da annobar Bayan shekaru uku na cutar COVID-19 tsarin kiwon lafiya a cikin ƙasashe sun fara nuna alamun farko na farfaɗowar tsarin kiwon lafiya a cewar rahoton wucin gadi na hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) game da zagaye na huɗu na binciken bugun jini na duniya kan cigaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya a lokacin annobar COVID-19 Nuwaba 2022 January 2023. Ya zuwa farkon shekarar 2023, kasashe sun ba da rahoton samun raguwar cikas wajen isar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya na yau da kullun, amma sun nuna bukatar saka hannun jari don farfadowa da kuma karfin juriya na gaba. A cikin kasashe 139 da suka amsa zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kasashe sun ba da rahoton ci gaba da wargajewa a kusan kashi daya bisa hudu na ayyuka a matsakaici. A cikin ƙasashe 84 inda za a iya nazarin yanayin, yawan ayyukan da aka katse ya ragu a matsaikata daga 56% a watan Yuli-Satumba 2020 zuwa 23% a watan Nuwamba 2022- Janairu 2023. Ci gaba da rikice-rikicen ya faru ne saboda abubuwan buƙatu- da abubuwan wadata, gami da ƙarancin matakan neman kiwon lafiya a cikin al'ummomi gami da ƙarancin wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da sauran albarkatun kula da lafiya kamar buɗaɗɗen asibitoci ko samfuran magunguna da samfuran da ake samu. Abin maraba ne cewa tsarin kiwon lafiya a yawancin kasashe sun fara dawo da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya ga miliyoyin mutanen da suka rasa su yayin barkewar cutar," in ji Dr Rudi Eggers, Daraktan Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya. Amma muna buƙatar tabbatar da cewa dukkan ƙasashe suna ci gaba da rufe wannan rata don dawo da ayyukan kiwon lafiya, da kuma amfani da darussan da aka koya don gina shirye-shiryen kiwon lafiya da tsayayye don nan gaba". Alamun farko na samun lafiya A cikin wannan sabon binciken, ƙasashe kaɗan sun ba da rahoton ƙaddamar da damar samun dama ga duk dandamali na isar da sabis da mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiyar jama'a tun daga rahoton 2020-2021, suna nuna muhimmin mataki na komawa zuw matakin kafin annoba da isar da sabis na da faffadan tsarin aiki.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Sabon sakamakon bincike sun nuna tsarin lafiya na farawa da dawowa kan hanya bayan jarrabawar cutar COVID-19. Bayan shekaru uku na jarrabawar cutar COVID-19, tsarin lafiya a kasashe daban-daban sun fara nuna alama mafiya yawa ta dawowar tsarin lafiya, kamar yadda rahoton da aka yi a matsayin wani rahotan da aka yi a tsakiyar WHO kan “Binciken duniya na karo na hudu kan ci gaba da hidimomin lafiya na mahimmanci a lokacin jarrabawar cutar COVID-19: Nuwamba 2022 – Janairu 2023”.  A farkon shekarar 2023, kasashe sun bayyana cewa an rage tsangayawar a samar da hidimomin lafiya na yau da kullun, amma sun jaddada bukatar saka kuɗi don dawowa kan hanya da ƙarfi don gudanar da ayyukan lafiya a gaba.  A cikin kasashe 139 da suka amsa binciken WHO na karo na hudu, kasashe sun bayyana cewa kusan kwatar ɗaya na hidimomin lafiya na ci gaba da fuskantar tsangayoyi. A kasashe 84 inda za'a yi aiyukan bincike akan yawan canji, kashi na hidimomin da aka tsangaya ya ragu a matsakicin kashi 56% a tsakanin Yuli zuwa Satumba 2020 zuwa 23% a tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 zuwa Janairu 2023.  Tsangayoyin da ke ci gaba da kasawa suna da alaƙa da abubuwan da suka shafi bukatar hidimomi da samar da hidimomi, gami da ƙananan matakan neman hidimomin lafiya a cikin al'umma da kuma ƙuntatawar maƙasashi da sauran abubuwan da ke ba da hidimomin lafiya, irin su asibitai da ke da kaso ko adadi mai yawa na magani da kayan aiki.  "Murna ce cewa tsarin lafiya a yawancin kasashe ke farawa da dawowa kan hanyar mayar da hidimomin lafiya na mahimmanci ga miliyoyin mutane da suka rasa su a lokacin jarrabawar cuta," in ji Dr Rudi Eggers, Daraktan Hidimomin Lafiya na WHO. "Amma muna buƙatar tabbatar da cewa dukkan kasashe suna ci gaba da rufe wannan rashi don dawowa kan hanyar hidimomin lafiya, da kuma amsar ilmantarwa don gina tsarin lafiya da ke shirye da ƙarfi don gudanar da ayyukan lafiya a gaba."
